# QA Evidence

This document separates current verification evidence from historical captures.

## 2026-07-20 V1 G0 Decision And Assurance No-Network Checklist

- [x] `docs/v1/g0/decision-v1.json` is a closed, duplicate-key-rejecting source
  for the G0 status, baseline, product scope, release policy, security selection,
  privacy/operations floors, quality gates, CI tiers, blockers, source records,
  supersession rules, and next gate.
- [x] `docs/v1/g0/assurance-v1.json` and its human review companion pin 29
  source inputs and close the static protocol/data-flow inventory, T001-T026
  threat refresh, ten-risk register, nine-event observability allowlist, five
  decision-bound release-record classes, release checklist, seven incident
  classes, rollback runbook, and fourteen
  unassigned approval roles without opening implementation authority.
- [x] Its `g0ClosureContract` crosswalks all ten blockers to all nine G0 checks,
  all fourteen accountable roles, and exact gate-scoped evidence kinds. Owner,
  gate, evidence-catalog, and publication receipt fields and conditional
  bindings are closed. Receipt-derived trust anchors, partial bundles, and
  activation before a factory-only independently verified trust context are
  forbidden and mutation-pinned. The current checker exposes no receipt-
  acceptance API. A local dormant publication receipt candidate now exists
  outside the immutable V1/V2/V3 bytes, but every owner/gate/activation state
  and every blocker remains open.
- [x] `docs/v1/g0/assurance-checkpoint-readback-v1.json` captures the current
  assurance raw and canonical hashes plus an ordered 29-record source readback.
  `script/check_v1_g0_checkpoint.py` independently rehashes every exact regular
  non-symlink source with a 4 MiB ceiling, same-descriptor stability, and final
  path-identity recheck. It hash-checks the decision before parsing, bounds JSON
  integers to 128 digits, and pins the candidate bytes outside the record while
  retaining `candidate_observed_not_immutable`, absent owner acceptance and
  publication, all ten blockers, and false G1a/network/signing/deployment
  authority.
- [x] The human decision preserves Android/macOS and runtime-mediated
  Ollama/LM Studio, freezes the five launch locales, keeps P2P as a GA gate,
  retains TURN plus sealed emergency fallback, and moves Intel macOS and broad
  platform/backend/tool expansion to Post-V1.
- [x] Distribution is explicit without pretending credentials exist: Google
  Play closed-to-staged production with Play App Signing for Android, and direct
  Developer ID/hardened-runtime/notarized distribution for macOS. Production app
  IDs, account owners, certificates, and keys remain absent blockers.
- [x] Relay TLS signed leases, pair-epoch recovery, deny-only emergency revoke,
  fresh-QR replacement, typed route-authorization digests, privacy retention,
  and per-cell release measurements are selected as future requirements rather

  fetch capabilities are mandatory, revoked retained state has an absolute

  zero-allowance hard stops.
- [x] The release matrix names twelve non-omittable network cells, including
  immutable VPN and suspend/resume cohorts, plus six required variants for
  symmetric NAT, consent loss, deliberate P2P failure, fallback outages, and a
  regional outage. Native-IPv6 and home-NAT cells have a separate direct-P2P
  release threshold. Four measurement contracts bind targets to owner/source/
  sample/failure policy. Provider minimum versions, service domains, and named
  measurement owners remain explicit G0 blockers rather than inferred facts.
- [x] macOS V1 distribution is narrowed to a stapled signed DMG; PKG and its
  separate Developer ID Installer custody are not silently left as an option.
- [x] `python3 script/check_v1_g0_decision.py` passes and hash-verifies nine

## 2026-07-20 Android Runtime Session Summary Linear Merge No-Device Checklist

- [x] `PersistedRuntimeData.withRuntimeChatSessionSummaries` builds deleted ids once, creates one first-wins existing-session index, collects local-only rows and collision ids in the same persisted-session pass, and deduplicates incoming summaries once.
- [x] The merge passes the indexed existing row directly into `runtimeChatSession`; it does not call `deletedRuntimeSessionIds()` or `firstOrNull` once per summary. Duplicate legacy persisted ids preserve the previous `firstOrNull` first-wins meaning.
- [x] `runtimeSessionSummaryBatchMergeReadsPersistedStateLinearlyAndPreservesFirstWinsState` uses 1,003 existing rows and 1,001 suppression rows. It requires at most `existing size + 1` and `suppression size * 2 + 2` reads respectively and verifies manual title, draft, messages, model, message count, search rank/snippet/fields, local-only collision, and permanent deletion suppression.
- [x] Focused evidence: the new regression plus `runtimeSessionSummariesReplaceRuntimeOwnedCacheAndPreserveLocalSessions` and `permanentlyDeletedRuntimeSessionDoesNotReturnFromRuntimeSync` pass together.
- [x] Broad Android evidence: all 634 `RuntimeClientViewModelTest` tests pass. `build/qa/android-session-summary-linear-full-20260720.log` exits zero with `BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 30s`; the refreshed JUnit XML reports contain 1,141 app, 162 protocol, 95 transport, and 130 pairing JVM tests with zero failures, errors, or skips, and `:app:assembleDebug` succeeds.
- [x] Documentation guard repair: `python3 -m unittest script.test_documentation_handoff_guards` passes 11/11 after its Status fixture was updated from the removed Quick Actions pairing callback to the current `performRuntimeOverviewAction` contract. Callback swaps and comment/string decoys still fail closed.
- [x] Static pre-aggregate evidence: copy hygiene across 92 source/resource files, docs hygiene across 12 current documents, five-locale macOS localization parity, Python compilation, shell syntax, and `git diff --check` pass.
- [x] Final integrated no-device aggregate: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-session-summary-linear-final-20260720.log` exits zero across 8,806 lines in 580.459 seconds. It contains exactly one `No-device quality checks passed.` marker, one Android session-summary linear-merge marker, and one 143-filter Swift full-suite subsumption marker. Swift executes 1,809 tests with two explicit environment-dependent skips and zero failures; the Android selection includes the complete ViewModel class and its new regression.
- Agent state: two GPT-5.6 Sol read-only reviews compared repository-wide next candidates; the lower-risk Android batch merge was selected over the cross-security-boundary strict JSON parser allocation candidate. GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.
- Proof boundary: this is deterministic JVM/static/build evidence with no attached Android device. It does not prove device latency, physical process-death durability, camera QR, TalkBack, haptics, live-provider traffic, external networking, production relay/P2P, or deployment readiness.
- Verification commands: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="/Users/hanchangha/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`; `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="/Users/hanchangha/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:protocol:testDebugUnitTest :core:transport:testDebugUnitTest :core:pairing:testDebugUnitTest :app:testDebugUnitTest :app:assembleDebug -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`; `python3 -m unittest script.test_documentation_handoff_guards`; `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`; `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`; `python3 script/check_macos_localization.py`; `bash -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh` (syntax only); `git diff --check`.

## 2026-07-19 Canonical Session Handoff Documentation Checklist

- [x] `docs/handoff.md` is the explicit canonical first read and `README.md` links to it before historical implementation detail.
- [x] The handoff identifies the repository, branch, starting HEAD, intentionally dirty tracked worktree, pre-refresh absence of untracked files, final expected 44-modified-plus-two-untracked manifest/test baseline, no new commit/push, Android disconnected state, and ephemeral macOS process state. It requires live refresh rather than treating the snapshot as permanent.
- [x] The complete QR diagnosis is recoverable without conversation history: the clean development UI had no viable local fallback when remote material was absent, Android optical entry forced remote validation before its debug parser, Connection Recovery briefly shared a generic callback, and ad-hoc/LaunchServices startup behavior could block readiness.
- [x] The final design is mapped to concrete macOS core/UI/render files, Android optical policy/tests, the launch script/tests, static contracts, and synchronized docs. The UI callback matrix records Pairing main, Pairing recovery, Status quick action, Status recovery, toolbar, and menu-bar behavior without calling all of `PairingView` remote-only.
- [x] Physical evidence is separated from later Mac-only evidence. The handoff records the real `SM-S936N` camera scan without URI injection, `PairingQr` pairing/authentication/health, trusted admission, and `BonjourDiscovery` reconnect, while explicitly stating that later UI-only and launch-script changes did not rerun Android traffic after the phone was released.

It contains no full QR, device identifier, pairing material, or credential and is labeled as an observation manifest rather than replayable raw evidence.
- [x] Automated evidence wording preserves chronology: 525/525 full router tests before final remediations, final focused QR tests 5/5, localization 137/137, one exact Vision decode within the 15-configuration QR render, the 15-configuration primary-surface render, Android focused/release/install evidence, and final GPT-5.6 Sol P0-P2 closure.

The handoff requires inspecting the aggregate's final status before claiming it.
- [x] The Debug and Release evidence matrix states that the physical run used an Android debug APK and development macOS app. Release compile/policy checks passed, but release APK installation/camera pairing, installed distribution pairing, signing/notarization, and release-to-release operation were not performed.
- [x] Secret hygiene is explicit: the complete QR verifier output must not enter documentation, logs, commits, or chat; only safe fields and a digest may be recorded. Pairing codes, nonces, relay/allocation secrets, identity private material, provider URLs, and device credentials remain prohibited.
- [x] Dirty-worktree guidance distinguishes the QR/handoff file set from existing Android persistence/pairing durability and security-governance changes, and requires explicit file-list review before staging.
- [x] Not-proven and authority sections retain expiry/rotation, permission recovery, screen readers, broader devices, network handoff, unrelated-network pairing, external relay, P2P/NAT, Phase B, production, capacity, deployment, and live-provider boundaries.
- [x] The authority section links canonical P2P/NAT `progress-v8.json`, `decision-v6.json`, and `handoff-v9.json`; records both candidate rejections before compile, consumed one-shot acquisition authority, no selected library, and false implementation/compiler/socket/runtime-network permissions; and requires a new versioned review plus explicit user decision for another candidate.

The security-design validator and all seven Phase A progress tests pass while the whole review, compiler, socket, runtime-network, Phase B, production, and deployment gates remain closed; this is integrity evidence, not new authority.
- [x] Historical QR and readiness roadmap sections are marked superseded and use at-checkpoint wording, so their old no-device or remote-only state cannot override the current top sections.

The mutation tests reject swapped generic/remote callbacks even when both names remain, comment/string decoys, unknown secret fields, duplicate keys, full pairing URI variants, and digest drift. This is documentation/no-device verification only; no app rebuild, phone reconnection, optical rescan, or network/production run was performed for the refresh.

## 2026-07-19 macOS Debug Local QR And Android Optical Pairing Checklist

- [x] A clean debug environment without relay bootstrap or lease can request one explicit local-diagnostic pairing session through the same model-owned UI API used by Pairing, toolbar, menu-bar, Status, and startup actions. A complete remote route remains preferred.
- [x] The local fallback is available only in a debug assertion build, requires a validated nonloopback host and a runtime listener that has reached `advertising`, and does not alter the release/default `remoteRequired` APIs or the explicit remote-relay action. The release gate resolves false even when its constructor test hook requests true.
- [x] `testCompanionAppModelDebugUserInterfaceGeneratesLocalDiagnosticQRCodeWithoutRemoteRoute`, `testCompanionAppModelDebugUserInterfaceDoesNotGenerateQRCodeWhenRuntimeListenerFails`, and `testCompanionAppModelReleaseUserInterfaceDoesNotEnableLocalDiagnosticFallback` pin successful debug generation, fail-closed listener startup, and a non-overridable release gate.
- [x] `testCompanionAppModelDebugUserInterfaceUsesLocalDiagnosticAfterExplicitRemoteFailure` proves a failed explicit allocator attempt no longer traps the generic debug action in repeated remote retries: the next generic request creates a local QR and the allocator call count remains one. A complete ready remote route is still preferred.
- [x] `testCompanionAppModelLocalPairingInterfaceScorePrefersPrimaryPhysicalRoute` proves the SystemConfiguration primary IPv4 interface wins over other `en*` candidates while virtual interfaces remain excluded, reducing wrong-interface QR selection on multi-network Macs.
- [x] Connection Recovery's `Generate Latest QR` uses a separately named remote-only callback in `RemoteRelayRoutePanel`. `PairingView`'s main QR button calls `requestPairingForUserInterface()`, while its nested recovery panel calls `requestRemotePairingForUserInterface()` directly. `StatusView` and `ContentView` likewise keep the generic quick action separate from remote recovery. Copy hygiene extracts each Pairing, Status, toolbar, and menu callback block, requires the exact generic/remote call, rejects the opposite call, and ignores comment/string decoys.
- [x] The live-path rendered QR test requests the generic UI pairing path, renders its actual SwiftUI bitmap, and uses Vision to decode the exact compact payload. The physical live window separately exposed `pairing-active-qr`, and its full-screen QR decoded with complete required fields plus `direct_host=192.168.0.113`, `direct_port=43170`, and `route_scope=local_diagnostic`; payload SHA-256 was `efc77b1402ed6270b741e5ee69bb30a7527ad563876f58eee31e7587ef9544ef`.
- [x] Android `pairingQrRoutePolicyAllowsLocalDiagnosticOnlyInDebugBuilds` pins debug optical/deep-link admission and release remote-route enforcement. `compactLocalDiagnosticQrIsValidOnlyWhenRemoteRouteIsNotRequired` proves the compact local QR remains invalid under the release-style requirement.
- [x] `SM-S936N` camera scanning was used directly; no URI or deep-link injection was performed. Logs show source `PairingQr`, direct connection, `pairing.request`, `pairing.result`, hello, `auth.challenge`, `auth.response`, and `runtime.health`. macOS then reported one trusted device.
- [x] The sanitized physical observation manifest records Android 16/API 36, debug build, same-Wi-Fi topology, actual-screen camera scan, payload digest `efc77b1402ed6270b741e5ee69bb30a7527ad563876f58eee31e7587ef9544ef`, authentication/health/reconnect milestones, and discarded raw artifacts without retaining the device identifier or QR credentials.
- [x] After the core route remediations and verified macOS app build, Android force-stop and relaunch resumed the app in the foreground, discovered `192.168.0.113:43170` through `BonjourDiscovery`, completed challenge-response authentication with stored trust, and received `runtime.health`. Later UI-only callback and Mac launch-script changes did not rerun device traffic after the phone was released.
- [x] Swift evidence includes 525/525 complete router tests before the final review remediations, 5/5 focused final policy/route tests, 137/137 localization tests, the 15-configuration live-path QR render with one exact Vision decode, the 15-configuration primary-surface/Connection-Recovery render, and a successful release build. Android debug policy tests, `:app:assembleRelease` including `lintVital`, final `:app:installDebug`, and physical launch passed.
- [x] `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, `python3 script/check_macos_localization.py`, `python3 -m unittest script.test_build_and_run`, `bash -n script/build_and_run.sh`, `git diff --check`, and `./script/build_and_run.sh --verify` pass at the recorded checkpoints. The launcher `--verify` step itself proves only exact-PID liveness after its settle delay; separate checks observed responsiveness, port 43170, `pairing-active-qr`, and a screen QR decoding to `192.168.0.113:43170` with `local_diagnostic` scope. The debug identity file is owner-only and no QR secret is stored in repository evidence.
- [x] Final GPT-5.6 Sol review reports no remaining P0-P2 finding after the release gate, failed-remote recovery, primary-interface selection, and Connection Recovery callback remediations.
- Proof boundary: evidence is one same-Wi-Fi debug optical pairing and trusted reconnect on one physical Android device. It is not expiry/retry, real TalkBack/VoiceOver, different-network, production relay, external-network reliability, P2P/NAT, Phase B, deployment, or production-readiness proof.

## 2026-07-19 Android Bounded Volatile Persistence No-Device Checklist

- [x] Composer typing and valid active nonterminal `chat.delta` are the only coalesced persistence call sites. UI and memory publish immediately, while a dirty-only latest-state coordinator schedules one first-change flush within the 250 ms production maximum.
- [x] `volatilePersistenceCoordinatorCoalescesBurstAtExactMaximumDelay` proves 100 updates produce no early write, an update at 249 ms does not debounce the deadline, and the exact 250 ms flush writes the latest state once with volatile durability.
- [x] `immediatePersistenceCancelsStaleSnapshotAndCannotResurrectRemovedPairingSecret` proves an immediate barrier cancels the pending generation and a delayed job cannot restore deleted pairing metadata or its secret.
- [x] `composerDraftBurstFlushesOnLifecycleAndClearAndRestoresAfterRecreation` proves 100 draft mutations remain immediately visible, repeated lifecycle callbacks do not duplicate writes, pause/stop/save-state/clear flush durably, and recreation restores the latest flushed draft.
- [x] `chatDeltaBurstCoalescesAndDonePersistsLatestSnapshotExactlyOnce`, `cancelRequestFlushesPendingChatDeltaBeforeDispatch`, and `activeChatErrorSupersedesPendingDeltaWithoutDelayedRewrite` prove burst reduction plus durable terminal, cancellation, and error ordering with no delayed stale rewrite.
- [x] `malformedChatDoneFlushesPendingDeltaWithoutDelayedRewrite` and `localSendValidationErrorFlushesPendingDraftWithoutDelayedRewrite` prove malformed terminals and rejected local sends also supersede pending volatile state before publishing an error.
- [x] `durableSaveConfirmsSecretWriteMetadataAndOldSecretCleanup` proves new encrypted-secret confirmation precedes metadata commit and old-secret cleanup. `durableSaveRejectsSecretStoreWithoutConfirmationContract` proves a nonconfirming secret store fails before metadata admission. Metadata-commit failure compensation and cleanup-failure journal retry have separate injected-failure regressions.
- [x] Three trusted-route regressions prove secret-bound `relay-v2` rotation uses durable APIs only, failed new-secret persistence preserves previous metadata and secret, and failed old-secret cleanup remains journaled until the next trusted-route read succeeds.
- [x] `sameRouteSecretReplacementMetadataFailurePreservesPreviousSecret`, `unchangedPendingSecretIsNotRewrittenAcrossStateBarriers`, and `pairingStoreRePairDoesNotDrainJournaledCurrentSecret` prove secret-bound pending handles, no repeated encryption/write for an unchanged protected value, and current-reference exclusion during trusted cleanup retry.
- [x] `mainActivityRoutesNewEntryIntentsToOneTaskScopedStateWriter` pins `singleTask` and disabled document launching. Existing `onNewIntent` handling remains responsible for new launcher, deep-link, share, and diagnostic intents.
- [x] All 19 focused regressions pass. Android Studio JBR passes all 1,125 app JVM tests with zero skips, failures, or errors: 633 ViewModel, 15 local-store, and 158 navigation tests. Pairing passes 130/130, release Kotlin compilation succeeds, and the copy contract tracks 749 unique suite-subsumed selectors.

## 2026-07-18 v0.5 Model-Pull Audit Recovery Integrity No-Device Checklist

- [x] `recoverUnfinished(at:)` runs `validateAllRecordHistories` within the same `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction before `readUnfinishedRecords`, covering completed and unfinished audit histories before recovery or new intake can proceed.
- [x] v2 schema verification pins the exact `PRAGMA foreign_key_list` event-to-operation relation and `ON DELETE RESTRICT`. Recovery independently rejects both `PRAGMA foreign_key_check` rows and an events-to-operations orphan anti-join before streaming one operation-ordered `LEFT JOIN`.
- [x] Streaming retains at most one operation and three events instead of an unbounded ID array or N+1 full-history queries.
- [x] The durable audit cap is 10,000 operations. Exact-limit recovery succeeds in 0.265 seconds on the current host, while new intake at the limit returns `capacityExceeded`; this timing is no-device regression evidence, not production capacity proof.
- [x] `testRecoveryRejectsCorruptTerminalHistory` mutates a completed dismissal event timestamp in a valid v2 SQLite store and proves startup recovery returns `corruptPersistence` instead of silently succeeding.
- [x] `testRecoveryRejectsOrphanAuditEvent` inserts a valid-shaped orphan event with foreign keys disabled and proves recovery rejects it even though ordinary SQLite `quick_check` can remain clean.
- [x] `testRecoveryRejectsEventsSchemaWithoutRequiredForeignKey` rebuilds an otherwise equivalent v2 events table without only the required FK and proves schema opening fails closed.
- [x] `testRecoveryRejectsAuditHistoryAboveMaximumOperationCount` materializes 10,001 valid completed histories and proves startup recovery returns `capacityExceeded` before streaming or accepting new work; copy hygiene requires this exact named evidence in every aligned record.
- [x] The three broker startup tests preserve any unexpectedly accepted operation, attempt approval, and prove timestamp corruption, orphan data, and missing-FK schema drift map to `storageUnavailable`, no pending review, and zero provider dispatches.
- [x] The exact eight-test focused selector in `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` passes with zero failures.
- [x] All 43 `swift test --filter RuntimeModelPullApproval` tests and the adjacent 29 permission/coordinator tests pass with zero failures. Copy hygiene structurally pins the exact FK tuple, exact cap comparisons, over-limit regression, and each direct broker approval/zero-dispatch path; it passes across 91 files. Docs hygiene passes across 11 documents, and the P2P security-design validator passes its 57-file static preflight with all runtime-network and production gates closed.
- [x] Final GPT-5.6 Sol re-review reports `no P0-P3 findings` after the exact FK, capacity, broker direct-dispatch, over-limit evidence, and function-boundary guards were all tightened.
- [x] `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-model-pull-audit-recovery-integrity-final-20260718.log` exits 0 across 12,192 lines with exactly one `No-device quality checks passed.` marker and one dedicated model-pull audit-recovery marker. ## 2026-07-17 v0.2 Android Chat Sessions Bulk Terminal Authority No-Device Checklist

- Scope: Android JVM no-device proof for exact terminal authority on runtime-authoritative archive-all and delete-all. Request and response wire shapes are unchanged.
- Authority contract: success, malformed error, ordinary error, and send failure must match the exact pending request id, receiving or dispatch channel, connection generation, and authenticated authority generation; success and send failure also bind the lifecycle operation type.
- Focused command:

```bash
JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.chatSessionsBulkLifecycleRequiresExactTerminalAuthority \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.chatSessionsBulkMalformedCurrentErrorConsumesOnlyExactAuthority \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.chatSessionsBulkSendFailureRequiresExactDispatchAuthority
```

- Focused result: the exact three-selector JUnit XML records `tests=3`, `skipped=0`, `failures=0`, and `errors=0`.
- Adversarial result: `chatSessionsBulkLifecycleRequiresExactTerminalAuthority` injects current and prior-batch ids through a foreign channel, a prior connection generation, and a prior authentication generation. Success, `authentication_required`, ordinary, and malformed terminals remain inert; exact-current completion remains usable.
- Malformed result: `chatSessionsBulkMalformedCurrentErrorConsumesOnlyExactAuthority` proves exact-current unknown error metadata closes the pending operation with `invalid_payload`, starts reconciliation, and makes a late success inert.
- Send-failure result: `chatSessionsBulkSendFailureRequiresExactDispatchAuthority` proves an exact current send failure closes with `chat_session_sync_failed`, while delayed failures after channel, connection-generation, or authentication-generation replacement stay inert and do not prevent a later exact success.
- ViewModel result: Android Studio JBR `./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest'` passes all 597 tests with zero failures, errors, or skips.
- App result: Android Studio JBR `./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest` passes all 1,071 Android app JVM tests with zero failures, errors, or skips.
- Durable gate evidence: the default selector includes all three exact regressions, and `check_android_chat_sessions_bulk_terminal_authority_junit` parses generated JUnit XML to require one executed testcase per canonical name with no skip, failure, or error before emitting the dedicated no-device marker. Copy hygiene checks the production matcher and `chat-sessions-bulk-` namespace on success, malformed-error, ordinary-error, and send-failure paths plus the executable tests/helper, selectors, exact JUnit tuple, marker, protocol text, and all three documentation sections.
- Final aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-chat-sessions-bulk-terminal-authority-final-reviewed-20260717.log` exits 0 across 12,089 lines with one overall success marker, one dedicated bulk terminal-authority marker, one dynamic JUnit proof marker, two zero-delay registry self-tests, 88 local development-relay match lines, freshness across 56 authenticated relay connections, and 905 encrypted frame bodies.

JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest \

## 2026-07-17 v0.2 Android Authenticated Transcript And Document Read Authority No-Device Checklist

- Scope: Android JVM no-device state-machine proof for exact-current `chat.messages.list`, `index.documents.list`, and `retrieval.query` result, error, send-failure, and lifecycle authority. The wire request/response shapes are unchanged.
- Authority contract: each pending operation captures the exact request id, channel object, connection generation, and authenticated authority generation. Wrong-channel, old-connection, prior-authentication, duplicate, superseded, and delayed terminals remain inert before pending state, transient document state, transcript publication, authentication state, or device persistence can change.
- Focused command:

```bash
JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentCatalogRequiresExactCurrentAuthorityAndConsumesOnce \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentSearchRequiresExactCurrentAuthorityAndConsumesOnce \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.delayedOldDocumentSendFailureCannotCloseReplacementRequest \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.pendingDocumentAuthorityClearsOnDisconnectRevocationAndViewModelClear \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.chatMessagesListRequiresExactCurrentAuthorityAndConsumesOnce \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.sameChannelReauthenticationTombstonesOldChatMessagesAuthority \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentCatalogErrorConsumesOnlyExactCurrentAuthorityAllowsRetryAndKeepsDuplicatesInert \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentCatalogImmediateSendFailureAllowsRetryAndKeepsLateFramesInert \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.delayedOldChatMessagesListSendFailureCannotCloseReplacementRequest \
  --tests com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest.pendingChatMessagesAuthorityClearsOnDisconnectRevocationAndViewModelClear \
  -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain
```

- Focused result: the exact ten-selector JUnit XML records `tests=10`, `skipped=0`, `failures=0`, and `errors=0`. `runtimeDocumentCatalogRequiresExactCurrentAuthorityAndConsumesOnce` and `runtimeDocumentSearchRequiresExactCurrentAuthorityAndConsumesOnce` independently reject wrong channel, wrong connection generation, and prior authentication before accepting one exact-current result and ignoring its duplicate.
- Terminal-race result: `delayedOldDocumentSendFailureCannotCloseReplacementRequest` proves a replaced request's delayed send failure cannot close its replacement. Exact-current errors retain their existing bounded behavior, including one semantic-to-lexical retry only for the captured current `retrieval.query` payload.
- Lifecycle result: `pendingDocumentAuthorityClearsOnDisconnectRevocationAndViewModelClear` proves independent disconnect, authenticated-session revocation, and ViewModel clear removal. Production reauthentication and receive-failure closure use the same cleanup path.
- Transcript result: `chatMessagesListRequiresExactCurrentAuthorityAndConsumesOnce` proves only an exact-current result can publish and persist transcript messages; stale and duplicate results or errors remain inert. `sameChannelReauthenticationTombstonesOldChatMessagesAuthority` proves the old request remains closed across same-channel reauthentication while a replacement request succeeds.
- Direct catalog terminal evidence: `runtimeDocumentCatalogErrorConsumesOnlyExactCurrentAuthorityAllowsRetryAndKeepsDuplicatesInert` independently rejects wrong channel, old connection, and prior authentication, consumes one exact error, keeps its late error/result inert against a retry, and accepts the retry. `runtimeDocumentCatalogImmediateSendFailureAllowsRetryAndKeepsLateFramesInert` proves immediate send failure closes only its request and cannot let late success or authentication-looking error disturb the replacement.
- Direct transcript terminal evidence: `delayedOldChatMessagesListSendFailureCannotCloseReplacementRequest` proves a suspended old send failure cannot close or error the replacement transcript or prevent its persistence. `pendingChatMessagesAuthorityClearsOnDisconnectRevocationAndViewModelClear` independently proves all pending fields, loading state, and exact correlation close on each lifecycle path.
- ViewModel command: Android Studio JBR `./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest' -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain` passes all 590 tests with zero failures, errors, or skips.
- App command: Android Studio JBR `./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain` passes all 1,064 Android app JVM tests with zero failures, errors, or skips.
- Durable gate evidence: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` selects all ten regressions, parses the generated JUnit XML immediately after the Android selection, and requires exactly one executed testcase per expected name with no skip, failure, or error before emitting `Covered v0.2 addendum: Android authenticated transcript and document read current-request authority.` Copy hygiene pins the implementation, request-bound send failures, helper surfaces, tests, selectors, dynamic JUnit proof, marker, and matching documentation sections.
- Review evidence: the pre-implementation GPT-5.6 Sol audit reports no P0-P3 production finding. The first evidence review found one P3 raw-name copy-guard false-pass risk, and the first remediation review found one additional P3 because source regex alone could accept ignored or commented tests. The final evidence review found one P3 because copy hygiene did not pin every canonical name into the dynamic JUnit tuple. The executable `@Test` guard, exact ordered unique tuple comparison, and generated JUnit XML execution check remediate all three findings. The final delta re-review reports no P0-P3 findings.

## 2026-07-17 v0.2 Android Persistent-Memory Mutation Current-Request Authority No-Device Checklist

- [x] Request namespaces are `memory-upsert-<UUID>` and `memory-delete-<UUID>` for the existing authenticated `memory.upsert` and `memory.delete` operations.
- [x] Pending correlation binds exact operation; `NewEntry` exact trimmed content or `ExistingEntry` exact id; expected upsert `entry.id` when known, exact `entry.content`, exact `entry.enabled`, or expected delete `id`; exact channel object; connection generation; and authenticated runtime-authority generation. The tests decode each transmitted outbound payload and compare it to those exact values.
- [x] One exact-current result, request-specific error, or request-bound send failure consumes its record and timeout exactly once before publication. Malformed, unknown-metadata, and operation/target/expected-field-mismatched exact terminals also consume; late/duplicate/stale and wrong-channel/connection/authentication terminals are inert. Production-ingress coverage proves the receive-loop-captured source channel and connection generation are not re-stamped as current authority.
- [x] Same-target mutations are serialized while pending, while independent targets can remain pending and close independently.
- [x] Mutation dispatch invalidates any older pending `memory.list`, and an exact mutation terminal invalidates a distinct full-list request started during the mutation. Tests deliver the pre-mutation response before the terminal and the distinct during-mutation response after success, proving stale full-list responses cannot erase an add, revert an enable change, or resurrect a delete.
- [x] The production local timeout is 15 seconds; timeout closes only the exact mutation, publishes a bounded local error, requires a fresh unqueried `memory.list`, performs no automatic mutation retry, and leaves a late terminal inert. An independent sibling mutation defers the reconciliation until its terminal; invalidation preserves the requirement and reissues it after the final sibling closes.
- [x] Disconnect, receive failure, successful reauthentication, authentication revocation, and ViewModel clear cancel timeout jobs and clear pending mutation authority; delayed retired-authority send failures cannot affect a replacement.
- [x] Focused proof passes exactly five explicitly selected JVM tests: `memoryUpsertResultRejectsUnknownMetadataBeforeMemoryMutation`, `memoryDeleteResultRejectsUnknownMetadataBeforeMemoryMutation`, `memoryMutationResultsRequireExactCurrentAuthorityAndExpectedPayload`, `memoryMutationErrorsRequireExactCurrentAuthorityAndConsumeOnce`, and `memoryMutationSendFailureAndLifecycleCleanupRequireExactAuthority`.
- [x] Current-source Android evidence records 600 `RuntimeClientViewModelTest` tests and 1,074 app JVM tests with zero skips, failures, or errors. `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-memory-mutation-authority-final-reviewed-20260717.log` exits 0 across 12,101 lines with one overall success marker, one dedicated authority marker, one dynamic five-test JUnit proof marker, one dynamic production-deadline JUnit proof marker, two zero-delay registry self-tests, 88 local development-relay matches, freshness across 56 authenticated connections, and 905 encrypted frame bodies.
- [x] Review evidence records the remediated stale-list overwrite/toggle-binding findings plus later sibling-timeout reconciliation, stale-list false-pass, production-ingress, transmitted-payload, and deadline-execution evidence gaps. Deferred/reissued reconciliation, behaviorally observable list responses, old-channel ingress, decoded outbound payloads, and generated deadline XML proof close them. The final GPT-5.6 Sol re-review reports no remaining P0-P3 finding, and the refreshed aggregate closes the stale-evidence P3.
- [x] Scope remains Android-local acceptance/lifecycle hardening with no protocol message, schema/field, capability, host mutation semantics, provider route, client execution authority, socket/network authority, controlled-spike Phase B, production networking, or deployment change.
- [x] Evidence is explicitly no-device only. It does not establish physical Android, optical QR, peer receipt or exactly-once host mutation, real lost-response recovery beyond deterministic timeout, live-provider/live-network behavior, production relay/P2P/NAT, Phase B, or deployment.

The old request's send failure and later `authentication_required` error neither publish an error nor revoke the current session, while the replacement health result remains accepted.

## 2026-07-17 v0.2 Fail-Closed Resource-Bounded Provider Model-Catalog Trust Boundary

- Scope: no-device resource-bound, parser, catalog-identity, metadata, schema/Android decode, router-publication, and conservative-compaction proof for the existing Ollama and LM Studio model-list shape. No provider route, socket/network, Phase B, production, or deployment authority is added.
- Streaming evidence: injected limits exercise exact and limit-plus-one response bodies for Ollama catalog and show reads and both LM Studio catalog shapes. Separate fixtures provide oversized positive `Content-Length`; production code checks it before iteration and otherwise stops after the first byte over the 4 MiB ceiling. These are URLProtocol fixtures, not live-provider traffic.
- Count/fanout evidence: Ollama accepts exactly 256 rows and 256 detail calls, rejects 257 rows, rejects a 128-installed plus 129-running unique set before any detail call, and propagates task cancellation from a suspended show request. LM Studio accepts exactly 256 native rows and rejects 257 without fallback; unload accepts exactly 256 unique loaded-instance ids, while 257, duplicate, blank, canonically equivalent, or 513-code-point ids fail before POST fanout and also fail during polling. The router separately accepts exactly 256 aggregate rows and rejects 257 without partial publication.
- Metadata and context-window trust-boundary evidence: Swift, schema, and Android exact/plus-one tests cover 512-code-point ids/names/provider ids/remote ids, a 522-code-point qualified id, 32 byte-exact-after-decoding unique content-bearing capability values of 128 code points, exact size through `Int64.max`, and context integers through 16,777,216. One fixed list covering ASCII controls/space, Unicode separators, U+200B, and U+FEFF defines blank-only rejection on all three platforms; NFC/NFD-distinct capability values remain valid. Blank, exact-duplicate, padded-overlong, and over-ceiling samples fail closed. Existing `Decimal`, `NSDecimalRound`, duplicate/escape-equivalent key, identity alias, NFC/canonical, and precision-rounding fixtures remain selected.
- Fallback/stream evidence: LM Studio catalog fallback remains only 404, 405, or 501 and is not reached after oversized/malformed data, transport/auth failure, or other status. A final native `chat.end` JSON line without a trailing blank separator succeeds through `parser.finish()`; malformed partial output and terminal-less EOF still fail without a second dispatch.
- Router/compaction evidence: final publication rejects any invalid non-nil injected metadata as sanitized `bad_backend_response`, preserves `Int64.max` as the exact decimal `9223372036854775807`, and rejects the all-fields-at-maximum Cartesian catalog before its plaintext plus 16-byte relay tag can exceed the 1 MiB frame. Exact 1,048,560-byte plaintext passes the codec guard and 1,048,561 bytes fails. Ollama context-only rejection may omit that optional field while retaining validated capabilities; LM Studio invalid context rejects the catalog, and genuinely absent metadata retains the conservative legacy compaction fallback.
- Public single-flight evidence: `build/qa/swift-focused-model-catalog-single-flight-final-reviewed-20260717.log` records seven focused tests with zero failures in 0.436 seconds. Up to eight concurrent public waiters share one provider operation and a ninth gets sanitized retryable `backend_unavailable`; accepted results retain distinct request ids. A canceled non-last waiter returns before provider completion and sixteen sequential cancellation churn iterations do not add another provider call. Last-waiter cancellation stops provider work, a retiring canceled operation blocks replacement until it completes, and success or failure is not cached. Reauthentication suppresses only the stale publication through its per-waiter authority check. Internal authority catalog lookups remain outside coalescing by the single public coordinator call-site guard.
- Single-flight proof boundary: this is deterministic no-device mock-backend and Swift concurrency evidence. It does not establish live-provider behavior, physical-device behavior, external-network capacity, throughput, production relay/P2P, NAT traversal, or Phase B readiness.
- Current focused command: `build/qa/swift-focused-provider-catalog-resource-bounds-20260717.log` records 119 tests with two expected opt-in localhost live-provider skips and zero failures. It includes the exact relay boundary, all Ollama and LM Studio tests, aggregate cancellation, and model-list router publication/compaction checks.
- Current full command: `build/qa/swift-full-provider-catalog-single-flight-final-reviewed-20260717.log` records all 1,615 Swift tests with the same two expected skips and zero failures in 350.641 seconds, including 120 localization and 15 render-smoke tests.
- Android/static commands: Android Studio JBR `./gradlew :core:protocol:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.localagentbridge.android.core.protocol.ProtocolCodecTest' --rerun-tasks` records 133 tests with zero failures, errors, or skips. `python3 script/check_protocol_schema.py`, `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, Python compilation, `zsh -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh`, and `git diff --check` pass.
- Review remediation evidence: GPT-5.6 Sol found cancellation absorption, unbounded loaded-instance POST fanout, aggregate frame overflow, `size_bytes` precision loss, Unicode blank drift, stale integrity pins, identity/capability uniqueness drift, LM Studio unload selector gaps, documentation precision gaps, and canceled non-last waiter Task accumulation. Current implementation, tests, selectors, and durable guards remediate them. The final continuation-based delta re-review reports no P0-P3 findings.
- Integrity evidence: the closed P2P/NAT collection is `6e6dfbfc0cdb70370c30f54222584b69042a6e22b6df04c7f3e65043c38522bd`; the still-unselected production-relay collection is `e188e1b885419e376b9dcea85282b4aafb1d48692b134edc13aff2eedfbf6b66`; and the still-unselected Runtime-Python manifest/review hashes are `5d306ba9e53824a5934fc4e77ea767fdc43a644ef0c24dbd3dd943b99cebb6f6` and `ae42c42dac52fe82c0de09675d4ca51ce4dc3ac45e52e8c6266484a8dd841e75`. All three validators pass while socket/network, execution, protocol activation, Phase B, production networking, and deployment remain closed.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-model-catalog-single-flight-final-reviewed-20260717.log` exits 0 across 12,064 lines with one `No-device quality checks passed.` marker, one dedicated resource-bounded provider-catalog marker, one dedicated bounded public `models.list` single-flight marker, two zero-delay registry self-tests, 88 local development-relay match log lines, `Relay session nonce, ephemeral key, and transport binding freshness verified across 56 connections.`, and `Relay ciphertext boundary verified across 905 encrypted frame bodies.` Previous 87-test, 1,579-test, 12,063-line, 11,988-line, and older integrity captures are not reused.

It does not prove a physical device, optical QR, live Ollama/LM Studio, peer receipt, external networking, production relay/P2P, NAT traversal, Phase B, performance, capacity, deployment, or production readiness. All four controlled-spike approvals remain bounded Phase A.

## 2026-07-16 v0.2 Host-Local Chat Compaction Calibration Acceptance Report

- Scope: no-device proof for a bounded host-local aggregate over existing provider-reported chat-compaction usage records, plus an explicit localized macOS review surface, without protocol, Android, provider-call, automatic-policy, socket, or network expansion.
- Pure aggregation command: `swift test --filter RuntimeChatCompactionCalibrationReportTests` passes six tests. It covers exact four-field grouping, 20-sample review floor, hard-budget warning precedence above and below that floor, newest-first 1,000-sample cap, 32-group cap and omitted counts, deterministic ordering, structural ineligibility, relation counts, exact encoded key sets, and timestamp/content/id privacy canaries.
- Store command: `swift test --filter SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests` passes all 73 JSONL/SQLite tests. The current combined run records those store tests in 5.423 seconds. The new regressions prove that 1,100 newer calibration-shaped but ineligible rows do not consume the 1,000-sample window, selected records still require exact request binding, deterministic-preview estimates remain request-equal, duplicate exact terminals fail on append/reopen even when one duplicate has no calibration, session/request whitespace variants do not bind, malformed/wrong-type calibration fails closed, all four production scan ceilings are pinned and exercised through the same injected-limit paths, and old unrelated invalid data outside a completed bounded window is not decoded.
- Store-resource boundary: JSONL reverse-tail work stops at 64 MiB, 50,000 lines, or 4 MiB for one line, and a selected request outside that tail fails the report. SQLite scans at most 50,000 newest terminal rows, retains at most 1,000 fully eligible samples, and performs at most 1,000 indexed normalized-owner plus exact-session/request lookups and 1,000 two-row uniqueness checks. Scan exhaustion fails the report; no partial aggregate is presented as complete.
- Host command: `swift test --filter LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testCompanionAppModelPublishesCompactionCalibrationReportOnExplicitRefresh` passes and proves the report remains empty until explicit refresh, the store read does not execute on the main thread, success publishes the aggregate, and a later store failure clears prior groups, exposes the error, and ends the loading state.
- Localization command: `python3 script/check_macos_localization.py` reports parity for five locales, and `AetherLinkLocalizationTests/testCompactionCalibrationCopyLocalizesAcrossSupportedLanguages` passes the count, state, and wire-mode copy.
- Render command: `AetherLinkRenderSmokeTests/testCompactionCalibrationSheetRendersAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances` passes in English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French across System/Light/Dark appearance with nonblank opacity and color-variation checks.
- Combined command: the dedicated report/store/model/localization/render selector passes 82 tests with zero failures in 6.697 seconds.
- Review evidence: GPT-5.6 Sol review findings covering request-bound estimates, mixed calibrated/uncalibrated duplicate terminals, bounded store work, off-main publication, stale-result clearing, warning priority, privacy, and ceiling exhaustion are remediated. Final GPT-5.6 Sol re-review reports no P0-P3 findings. The production ceiling constants use the tested injected-limit paths, but the full production-scale workloads are not directly allocated in tests.
- Full Swift evidence: `build/qa/swift-full-chat-compaction-calibration-report-final-reviewed-20260716.log` records 1,554 tests executed with two expected live-provider opt-in skips and zero failures in 346.303 seconds.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-chat-compaction-calibration-report-final-reviewed-20260716.log` exits 0 across 11,916 lines with one `No-device quality checks passed.` marker, one dedicated host-local calibration-report marker, 88 loopback development-relay matches, and `Relay ciphertext boundary verified across 905 encrypted frame bodies.` It also records all four controlled-spike recommendations approved for bounded Phase A with the socket gate closed.
- Integrity evidence: the changed `CompanionAppModel.swift` refreshes the closed P2P/NAT 13-artifact collection to `61bf5182935fb9da7a2de0a92d1d2f3f534aeb9847fc410c7e44b2fc12846b31`. Its validator passes with bounded Phase A intact and the socket/network/Phase B gate closed.
- Privacy boundary: the encoded report contains exact provider/model/wire/estimator keys and integer counts only. It excludes prompt/messages, summaries, owner/session/request/event ids, timestamps, source pointers, endpoints, and protocol/client fields.
- Interpretation boundary: `ready_for_review` means only that 20 samples exist for manual inspection. It is not provider-tokenizer parity, a statistical acceptance threshold, provider quality evidence, or permission to change the conservative estimator. One `exceeded_input_budget` sample remains a warning at any count.
- Proof boundary: current evidence is synthetic Swift events, temporary JSONL/SQLite stores, and macOS offscreen rendering. It is not live Ollama/LM Studio acceptance evidence, physical Android, optical QR, peer receipt, external networking, production relay/P2P, NAT traversal, measurement, or deployment proof.

- Approval boundary: no wire/schema/capability, client execution, provider, permission, source, process/file authority beyond existing host reads, socket/network, controlled-spike Phase B, production networking, or deployment authority changed.

## 2026-07-16 v0.2 Runtime Model Idle-Unload Policy User Control

- Scope: no-device proof for a persisted runtime-host-local 5/10/30 minute model idle-unload policy, safe dynamic timer replacement, and localized macOS control without expanding client or network authority.
- Policy-store/queue evidence: `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter RuntimeModelIdleUnloadPolicyTests` passes four tests with zero failures. It pins ordered preset values, exact 300/600/1,800-second delays, the 10-minute default, known-value restoration, unknown-string fallback, and `testPolicyUpdateQueueSerializesConcurrentChangesAndIdentifiesLatest` proves a blocked first apply prevents a second apply from overtaking it while only the second completion is latest.
- Backend evidence: `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter AggregatingLlmBackendResidencyTests` passes all 33 tests with zero failures. `testUpdatingIdlePolicyUnloadsModelWhenNewDelayAlreadyElapsed` preserves elapsed idle time; `testUpdatingIdlePolicyWhileGenerationIsInFlightDefersUnloadUntilCompletion` protects active work; `testPendingIdleUnloadBlocksSameModelChatUntilProviderUnloadCompletes` blocks same-model provider dispatch during unload; `testCancelledChatWaitingForSameModelUnloadDoesNotReserveOrDispatch`, `testCancelledChatWaitingForModelSwitchUnloadDoesNotReserveOrDispatch`, and `testCancelledEmbeddingWaitingForSameModelUnloadDoesNotDispatch` prove cancelled same-model/cross-model waiters cannot reserve or dispatch; and `testExtendingIdlePolicyInvalidatesEarlierTimer` waits for an explicit stale-callback acknowledgement before asserting the replacement timer remains authoritative.
- Composition evidence: `testCompanionAppModelAppliesPersistedPolicyToInjectedAggregate` passes and proves a restored 30-minute policy overwrites an injected aggregate's prior effective delay to 1,800 seconds.
- Localization evidence: `python3 script/check_macos_localization.py` reports parity for five locales. `testModelIdleUnloadPolicyPickerUsesSelectedLanguage` pins the label, all three minute options, supported selection value/hint, and unsupported `Unavailable` value/provider reason in English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French.
- Render evidence: `testModelIdleUnloadPolicyPickerRendersAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances` passes in 34.275 seconds for the exact picker in supported and unsupported states across five languages and System/Light/Dark appearance. It checks dimensions, opacity samples, and color variation in a 360x96 offscreen bitmap; it does not prove typography clipping absence, control semantics traversal, physical display, or VoiceOver hardware behavior.
- Full Swift evidence: `build/qa/swift-full-runtime-model-idle-unload-policy-final-reviewed-20260716.log` records all 1,504 current-source tests passing with zero failures in 318.645 seconds, including all 14 macOS render smokes and the existing companion, transport, relay, protocol, and storage suites.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-runtime-model-idle-unload-policy-final-reviewed-20260716.log` exits 0 across 11,678 lines with one `No-device quality checks passed.` marker, one `Covered v0.2 addendum: runtime model idle-unload policy user control.` marker, two zero-delay registry self-tests, 88 loopback development-relay matches, and `Relay ciphertext boundary verified across 905 encrypted frame bodies.`
- Review evidence: the GPT-5.6 Sol review sequence found six P2 implementation/evidence issues and six P3 test/guard/evidence issues in total. Current remediation serializes provider unload and policy updates, defers cross-model admission until switch unload completion, makes unload waits cancellation-aware, aligns injected aggregates, replaces wall-clock timer assumptions with an acknowledged callback, exposes state-aware accessibility, renders the exact picker, and pins its wiring. Final core read-only re-review reports no P0-P3 findings; final UI confirmation reports no remaining code, localization, render, selector, or guard defect.
- Gate evidence: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` selects the policy-store/queue suite, seven dynamic/concurrency/cancellation regressions, the direct picker render, and injected-aggregate integration test in addition to its existing full localization/render selections, then emits `Covered v0.2 addendum: runtime model idle-unload policy user control`. Copy hygiene requires the same implementation, tests, docs, selectors, and marker.

The existing `runtime.health.model_residency.idle_unload_delay_seconds` field reports the current host delay; no request field or client policy authority was added. The persisted value is one closed enum with unknown values falling back to the default.
- Integrity evidence: the changed `CompanionAppModel.swift` evidence line refreshes the closed P2P/NAT 13-artifact collection to `2f1936f36c4945f74173477eb0ab65736bb0236fa2aca0eff7d0e3e210f90459`. The production P2P/NAT validator passes with bounded Phase A intact and its socket gate closed; production-relay and unselected Runtime-Python validators also pass.

## 2026-07-16 v0.5 Memory-Summary Durable Terminal Decision Linearization

- Scope: no-device Swift store/router evidence that approval and dismissal are one durable owner-and-draft-scoped terminal decision across retries, independent store instances, process boundaries, and wall-clock rollback.
- Store command: `swift test --filter RuntimeMemoryStoreSummaryDecisionTests` passes six tests with zero failures. The suite proves one commit in a same-process approve/dismiss race; a child Python process holding the real coordination lock, appending dismissal, and forcing a waiting Swift approval to conflict; both opposite-decision orders after reopen; append-free dismissal retry; rollback-timestamp edit/disable preservation; and rollback-timestamp delete non-resurrection.
- Router command: a five-test selector covering approval, dismissal, both SQLite source-lock spans, and store-error mapping passes with zero failures. Approval retry returns the later edited/disabled entry; opposite decisions return `memory_summary_draft_unavailable`; arbitrary store failures return fixed `memory_store_unavailable` text without their raw messages.
- Affected command: `swift test --filter 'RuntimeMemoryStoreSummaryDecisionTests|RuntimeMemoryStoreGeneratedDraftTests|RuntimeMemorySemanticEmbeddingCacheTests|RuntimeMemoryExactDuplicateSuggestionsTests|RuntimeSemanticMemorySearchTests|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testMemorySummaryDraft'` passes 98 tests with zero failures in 4.676 seconds.
- Ordering evidence: JSONL decode retains physical line ordinal and does not sort events by timestamp. The direct tests append edit and delete events with timestamps earlier than approval, reopen through a second store, and prove the later physical mutation remains authoritative.
- Process evidence: the helper process acquires the exact `.<event-log>.coordination.lock` with `fcntl.lockf`, signals readiness, writes and fsyncs a canonical dismissal event while the Swift approval is blocked, then releases. The Swift approval returns terminal conflict and the reopened log contains exactly one dismissal line and no approved entry.
- Fail-closed evidence: stores that do not explicitly implement atomic approval receive `memorySummaryDraftTerminalDecisionUnsupported`; the default cannot silently call generic upsert. Router conflict mapping stays distinct from sanitized non-terminal persistence failure mapping.
- Full Swift command: `swift test` exits 0 in `build/qa/swift-full-memory-summary-terminal-decision-final-reviewed-20260716.log`, with all 1,490 tests passing and zero failures in 286.018 seconds.
- Review evidence: the first GPT-5.6 Sol review reported three P2 findings for unsafe default approval, timestamp replay, and raw store errors, plus one P3 cross-process test gap. All four are remediated; final read-only re-review reports no remaining P0-P3 findings.
- Gate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-memory-summary-terminal-decision-final-reviewed-20260716.log` exits 0 across 11,618 lines with exactly one `No-device quality checks passed.` marker, one dedicated terminal-linearization marker, two zero-delay registry self-tests, 88 loopback development-relay `matched relay_id=` records, and `Relay ciphertext boundary verified across 905 encrypted frame bodies.` Copy hygiene pins implementation, tests, docs, selector, and marker.
- Integrity evidence: the changed Router source pin refreshes the closed P2P/NAT 13-artifact manifest to `61bf5182935fb9da7a2de0a92d1d2f3f534aeb9847fc410c7e44b2fc12846b31` and the unselected Runtime-Python manifest/review to `d0cd4bae9f4172ceab9fa861ae94711ec2d74de57bfd099980dac519ce603783` / `53fcaf96dd294340bda34cfa50f3445bb1b2d4c005d8915342b0332ea87193ac`. P2P sockets stay closed; Python execution and protocol activation stay false.
- Device state: Android SDK `adb devices -l` lists no attached device after the aggregate, so no physical-device claim is made.
- Proof boundary: no-device SwiftPM, temporary stores, and a test-only local process only; not optical QR, peer receipt, live provider, external network, production relay/P2P, NAT traversal, measurement, or deployment proof.
- Approval boundary: no wire/schema/capability, permission, provider/source, Python product feature, process/file/socket/network, Phase B, production networking, or deployment authority changed.

## 2026-07-16 v0.5 Memory-Summary Source-Bound Review Identity And Commit Linearization

- Scope: no-device Swift policy/router/store evidence for binding a review draft to its displayed source and linearizing approval or dismissal persistence against chat-source changes.
- Identity evidence: `testDraftIDBindsSessionMetadataAndVisibleSourceButNotAdvancingInactivity` mutates session id/title/model/last activity/message count, source count/range, pointer count/order/session/index/role/timestamp/excerpt, and preview while proving inactivity-only advancement is stable. It pins the exact domain-separated digest. The SQLite draft test repeats and reopens the database before comparing v2 ids.
- Pre-dispatch evidence: `testMemorySummaryDraftApproveRejectsRenamedSameCountSourceBeforeMemoryMutation` changes only the title after review. The old id returns `memory_summary_draft_unavailable`, writes no memory, and listing exposes a different v2 draft for the renamed session.
- Commit-race evidence: `testMemorySummaryDraftApproveRevalidatesSourceAtMutationCommit` and `testMemorySummaryDraftDismissRevalidatesSourceAtMutationCommit` pause after lookup, mutate the source, then prove the source-current transaction returns `memory_summary_draft_stale` with no approval or dismissal write.
- Lock and error evidence: `testMemorySummaryDraftApproveHoldsSQLiteSourceLockThroughMemoryMutation` and its dismiss counterpart pause inside the actual memory-store call, open a second store on the same database, require its append to fail with SQLite locked/busy, release the decision, and require the exact retry to succeed. `testMemorySummaryDecisionMutationErrorsRemainMemoryStoreErrors` now proves operation failures remain memory-store failures through sanitized `memory_store_unavailable`, rather than stale, chat-store, raw `internal_error`, or underlying exception text.
- Transition evidence: `testMemorySummaryDraftsListRequiresV2ReviewForLegacyRecordsAndRejectsLegacyRequestAuthority` exposes deterministic v2 drafts for historical approved, dismissed, and generated v1 records, rejects v1 generate/approve/dismiss requests, and uses a throwing model-list backend to prove both model-list and chat dispatch counts remain zero before any new decision mutation.
- Focused commands: the source-bound policy plus eight router regressions pass 15 checks; `swift test --filter 'RuntimeMemoryStoreGeneratedDraftTests|RuntimeLongInactivityMemorySummarizationPolicyTests'` passes 23 checks.
- Broader evidence: `swift test --filter 'LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testMemorySummary'` passes all 61 matching router checks in 4.454 seconds with zero failures.
- Smoke-race evidence: the first default aggregate reached the malformed-backend generation path after the dismiss draft had been listed, but a separately scheduled automatic title commit had already rotated its title-bound v2 id. The product correctly returned `memory_summary_draft_unavailable`. The corrected smoke acknowledges `smoke-memory-summary-dismiss-title-stabilize`, asserts `Memory summary dismiss smoke`, and only then lists the draft. `testMemorySummaryDraftGenerateRejectsStaleGuardsBeforeModelLookup` separately commits a title-only change and proves no model listing or backend dispatch occurs before the replacement draft is exposed.
- Relay evidence: `build/qa/runtime-authenticated-relay-smoke-memory-summary-title-stabilized-20260716.log` passes with 56 fresh authenticated connections and 905 encrypted frame bodies.
- Review evidence: GPT-5.6 Sol passes found weak-v1 suppression, pre-lookup model-catalog access, lock/error/request-matrix proof gaps, and independently reviewed the final smoke/test correction. All findings are remediated; final re-review reports `no P0-P3 findings`.
- Full Swift evidence: `build/qa/swift-full-memory-summary-source-bound-review-identity-final-reviewed-r2-20260716.log` records all 1,484 current-source tests passing with zero failures in 282.207 seconds.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-memory-summary-source-bound-review-identity-final-reviewed-r2-20260716.log` exits 0 across 11,605 lines with one `No-device quality checks passed.` marker, one dedicated source-bound marker, two zero-delay development-registry self-test markers, 56 local development-relay matches, and `Relay ciphertext boundary verified across 905 encrypted frame bodies.`
- Device state: Android SDK `adb devices -l` lists no attached device. No physical-device result is claimed.
- Proof boundary: no Android device is attached. This does not prove physical Android, camera/optical QR, peer receipt, live provider, external network, production relay/P2P, NAT traversal, measurement, or deployment, and it grants no new source, process, Python, socket, network, Phase B, or production authority.

## 2026-07-16 v0.5 Android Memory-Summary Request Deadline Closure

- Scope: no-device Android JVM virtual-time proof for bounded memory-summary request lifecycle without changing the protocol or activation boundary.
- Deadline evidence: production-created ViewModels enable a 15-second timeout for list, approve, and dismiss control requests and a 75-second timeout for generate. The host retains a 60-second whole-generation deadline. `memorySummaryListAndActionsTimeoutClosePendingAndAllowRetry` checks the control boundary at 14,999/15,000 milliseconds, keeps generation pending there, then closes generation at 75,000 milliseconds and permits fresh retries.
- Host-alignment evidence: `memorySummaryGenerationAcceptsResultAfterControlDeadlineBeforeHostAlignedDeadline` advances through the control boundary and accepts a valid generation result at 59,999 milliseconds, before the host's 60-second limit and Android's 75,000 milliseconds fallback, retains the exact Job object, and observes that Job become cancelled without a timeout error.
- Terminal-cancellation evidence: `memorySummaryProtocolErrorMalformedResultAndSendFailureCancelExactTimeoutJobs` enables both production-equivalent deadlines, retains each exact Job, and proves protocol error, malformed result, and send failure cancel their Jobs before clearing generation, approval, or dismissal state.
- Deferred-refresh evidence: `memorySummaryActionTimeoutDrainsDeferredRefreshOnceAndIgnoresLateTerminals` leaves three actions concurrent, requests a deferred refresh, advances the operation-specific deadlines, and observes exactly one replacement list. Late valid generate/approve/dismiss results plus an `authentication_required` error are inert: drafts, local storage, timeout error, list count, and authenticated authority remain unchanged.
- Authority/lifecycle evidence: `memorySummaryTimeoutJobsStayAuthorityBoundAndCancelOnDisconnectAndClear` crosses an old deadline after same-channel reauthentication while a replacement action remains pending, then proves disconnect and ViewModel clear cancel and remove all outstanding Jobs.
- Production evidence: `RuntimeClientViewModelProductionDeadlineTest.productionFactoryEnablesHostAlignedMemorySummaryDeadlines` executes the real dependency factory under Robolectric and verifies the 15-second control and 75-second generation values. Copy hygiene separately pins public-constructor delegation to that factory.
- Focused result: all six focused tests pass with zero failures under Android Studio JBR.
- Gate evidence: the default selector contains all six exact tests and emits `Covered v0.5 addendum: Android memory-summary request deadline closure`. Copy hygiene pins the production factory values and execution test, keyed Job map, scheduling/cancellation helpers, retained-Job assertions, five documentation surfaces, selectors, and marker.
- Broader Android evidence: 34 memory-summary ViewModel tests plus the production-factory test pass, all 575 `RuntimeClientViewModelTest` checks pass, and the complete 1,049-test Android app unit suite reports zero failures, errors, or skips after forced task re-execution.
- Review evidence: GPT-5.6 Sol found no product-code defect and no P0-P2 finding. Its P3 exact-cancellation evidence gap is covered by the retained-Job assertions and the new terminal/send-failure regression; final re-review reports `no P0-P3 findings`.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-android-memory-summary-request-deadline-final-reviewed-r2-20260716.log` exits 0 across 11,556 lines with one `No-device quality checks passed.` marker, one `Covered v0.5 addendum: Android memory-summary request deadline closure`, 88 local development-relay matches, 903 encrypted frame bodies, and two zero-delay mock registry self-tests. Static copy, docs, protocol-schema, shell-syntax, Python-syntax, security-design, and diff checks pass in the same closure run.
- Device state: Android SDK `adb devices -l` lists no attached device. No physical-device result is claimed.
- Proof boundary: Android JVM no-device evidence only, not optical QR, peer receipt, live-provider, external networking, production P2P/relay, NAT traversal, measurement, or deployment proof.
- Approval boundary: no wire/schema/capability or execution authority changed. Controlled-spike approvals remain bounded Phase A; source acquisition/execution, sockets/network, Phase B, production, and deployment remain closed, while runtime Python and semantic memory remain `proposed_not_selected`.

## 2026-07-16 v0.5 Android Memory-Summary Terminal Channel And Source-Identity Closure

Generated output text, method, timestamp, and model are not source identity and retain their separate result-metadata validation.
