## 2026-07-14 v0.2 Runtime-Authoritative Session Pagination And Bulk Lifecycle No-Device Checklist

- Protocol checklist: `chat.sessions.authoritative_sync.v1`; compatible legacy and capable `chat.sessions.list` response shapes; cursor-only continuation; `snapshot_count` from 0 through 10,000; `next_cursor` capped at 512 UTF-8 bytes; at most 200 unique session ids per page; and exact-shape archive/delete bulk request and result DTOs with 200-row batch bounds and RFC 3339 completion time.
- Host checklist: HMAC cursor binding covers connection, normalized owner, search context, page size, snapshot count, offset, and expiry. Snapshot TTL is 120 monotonic seconds with signed wall-clock canonicality, one snapshot per connection, eight globally, and a 10,000-session ceiling detected without silent truncation. Lifecycle, authentication, and latest-initial generations are captured and rechecked before initial publication; connection close, authentication challenge/cleanup, rename, and lifecycle changes invalidate snapshots. Tests cover clock rollback, same-connection reauthentication, suppressed in-flight publication, and old-slow/new-fast initial ordering.
- Android checklist: intermediate pages remain private until a validated terminal page. Duplicate ids, cursor loops, count drift, empty nonterminal pages, final mismatch, more than 100 pages, stale channels, stale authority, and authoritative-to-legacy downgrade fail closed while preserving the prior cache. A downgrade disables bulk authority until a fresh unqueried capable snapshot completes. Capable support is learned only from a valid unqueried capable list. Refresh supersedes list work; bulk work cannot be superseded by refresh. List and transcript send failures clear only their matching current operation, and stale delayed failures cannot revoke new authority.
- Bulk checklist: owner-scoped deterministic `all_active` archive and `all_archived` delete batches are bounded at 200. Each batch is atomic; exact delete targets are purged from the compaction-summary cache before lifecycle commit, SQLite uses transaction rollback, and JSONL performs coordinated atomic replacement. Android uses fresh request ids, 50-batch and 10,000-row ceilings, no optimistic runtime-owned mutation, mandatory failure reconciliation, and no automatic retry. Bulk restore is rejected, and legacy remote plus local-only offline paths remain compatible.
- Focused evidence: `ProtocolCodecTest` passes 96 tests. The selected Android app command passes 632 tests: 469 ViewModel, 10 mutation-failure, and 153 navigation tests, with zero skipped, failures, or errors. The direct no-device Swift authoritative selector passes 19 tests, and `swift test --filter SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests` passes all 55 tests.
- Review evidence: the first GPT-5.6 Sol review reported five P2/P3 gaps around reauthentication, initial ordering, downgrade, history cleanup, and elapsed TTL. A second review found four more authority gaps around awaited challenge lookup, lifecycle authority drift, downgrade fallback, and delayed closed history authentication errors. Later split reviews found request-id reuse masking, development mutation after connection closure, stale errors after bounded-correlation eviction, malformed stale UI mutation, current malformed transcript errors retaining pending state, and unbounded closed UUID tombstones. Subsequent reviews found rename authority drift, cleanup/cancellation ordering, authentication/history namespace collision, pre-registration dispatch, and backend generation cancellation racing request teardown. Exact rename authority revalidation, authentication-before-history-error precedence, a registration start barrier, and claim-backend/cancel-task/clear-lifecycle ordering now have direct regressions or static guards. The final non-delegating GPT-5.6 Sol-only re-review reports no P0-P3 findings; the macOS reviewer result that internally delegated to Spark is explicitly excluded.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-v02-authoritative-session-sync-final-reviewed-20260714.log` has 9,410 lines and exits 0 with `No-device quality checks passed.`, 55 passing SQLite tests, the 19-test authoritative selector, 761 encrypted local-relay frame bodies, `Covered runtime-authoritative chat session pagination and bulk lifecycle addendum`, all four bounded Phase A controlled-spike approvals, and the socket gate closed.
- Proof boundary: no-device Android JVM/fake-channel and macOS SwiftPM/JSONL/SQLite evidence only. It is not physical Android UI, optical QR, live-provider, production relay/P2P, ICE/STUN/TURN, NAT traversal, or real-network evidence.
- Agent state: accepted evidence is GPT-5.6 Sol-only. One intermediate Sol reviewer delegated to GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark against instruction; that output is excluded and replaced by a non-delegating Sol-only final review.

## 2026-07-14 v0.2 Deterministic Session Search And Search-Only Sync No-Device Checklist

- Android authority: only summaries from the exact current pending authenticated search response enter `runtimeChatSearchSummariesById`. Completed-response replay and old-channel responses are ignored; connection replacement, receive failure, request-specific authentication loss, route expiry, disconnect, and an unqueried authoritative refresh clear pending history plus the map and visible search state. The same authority revocation clears pending title/lifecycle/rename ids and reverses optimistic mutations by descending dispatch sequence, so a pending delete tombstone cannot survive authentication loss. Mutation ACKs must match the journaled request, session, and lifecycle operation; malformed/mismatched success rolls back, same-session mutations serialize, and rename rollback restores its exact timestamp. Replacement rejects stale request A and delayed old-channel send failure while accepting only fresh request B. A promoted summary and its visible row are consumed before later lifecycle actions; suppressed deleted ids still win, and local-only id collisions preserve their local title/transcript while excluding the colliding remote row from actionable search results.
- Android sync: selecting an uncached search-only row promotes exactly that summary in memory, preserves the existing bounded full-history cache, selects the row, marks transcript loading, and sends `chat.messages.list` for the exact session id. The returned transcript becomes active without storing query rank, snippet, or matched-field metadata. A rejected optimistic lifecycle mutation restores the previous session immediately, invalidates an in-flight search/list request, sends a fresh unqueried full refresh, and ignores the stale response.
- macOS ordering: base session projection uses `lastActivityAt DESC, sessionID ASC`; lexical JSONL and SQLite ranking uses `score DESC, lastActivityAt DESC, sessionID ASC` before applying `limit`.
- Focused Android evidence: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest --tests 'com.localagentbridge.android.runtime.RuntimeClientViewModelTest'` passes 446 tests with zero skipped, failures, or errors; all 10 `RuntimeClientChatSessionMutationFailureTest` tests also pass. The direct fourteen-test search-authority slice covers search-only open, completed-response replay rejection, receive-failure revocation, distinct-channel replacement with fresh request B plus stale request A, delayed revoked-session send-failure rejection after an actual same-channel authentication handshake, completed mutation send-failure rejection in the same authority generation, completed pre-auth pairing/hello send-failure rejection after authentication, history and memory authentication-loss revocation, mutation-failure rollback with stale pending-search supersession, cross-session archive/delete rollback plus late-ack rejection, visible-row/summary one-shot consumption, and local-only identity-collision preservation. The mutation class covers ACK/session/operation binding, malformed ACK rollback, same-session serialization, exact rename timestamp rollback, and stale pre-mutation-list supersession. Existing route-expiry regressions require authentication/pending-history revocation; transient search/full-cache and runtime transcript tests remain green in the full class.
- Focused macOS evidence: `swift test --filter SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests` passes 51 tests with no failures. A direct four-test slice covers JSONL/SQLite equal-rank ties, SQLite reopen, FTS rank/snippet behavior, and same-timestamp append ordering.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-v02-session-search-sync-final-reviewed-r2-20260714.log` has 9,214 lines and exits 0 with `No-device quality checks passed.`, 51 passing SQLite tests, 761 encrypted relay frame bodies, `Covered runtime session search addendum`, all four bounded Phase A controlled-spike approvals, and the socket gate closed. Final GPT-5.6 Sol re-review reports no P0-P3 findings.
- Proof boundary: no-device JVM/fake-channel and SwiftPM/JSONL/SQLite evidence only. It is not physical Android UI, live-provider ranking quality, optical QR, production relay/P2P, or real-network evidence.
- Agent state: GPT-5.6 Sol only; GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.

## 2026-07-13 macOS Runtime Chat Retention Production Ownership No-Device Checklist

- Production ownership: `CompanionAppModel.start()` schedules immediate runtime chat retention and repeats it every 24 hours. Structured utility-priority child work drains the current eligible backlog in cancellable batches without keeping a discarded model alive; only bounded state and content-free Activity outcomes return to the main actor, with no full-history summary rescan.
- All-owner storage boundary: ownerless `RuntimeChatEventStoreDefaults.runProductionMaintenance(on:now:policy:)` invokes SQLite all-owner pruning across legacy nil-owner and device-owner scopes. Each transaction uses metadata-only window SQL with `LIMIT 100`; deterministic ordering is deletion time, owner, then session id, and the model repeats batches until fewer than 100 sessions remain. The original owner-scoped overload remains covered.
- Retention safety: the 90-day/100-session production default removes only old sessions whose latest lifecycle event is `deleted`. Tests preserve active, archived, recent-deleted, and same-session-id/different-owner data, delete semantic and FTS rows only in the pruned scope, verify owner/session tombstones prevent resurrection, and prove legacy JSONL compaction runs once after the final committed batch while a concurrently queued current-writer append survives replacement and later backfill.
- Host UI: Runtime History Inspector displays the fixed policy and localized `Clean Deleted History` control, disables it while running, and provides content-free completion/empty/failure status plus accessibility hint/value across all five supported languages.
- Focused evidence: `swift test --filter 'SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testCompanionAppModelRunsAllOwnerRuntimeChatRetentionMaintenance|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testCompanionAppModelRetentionMaintenanceDoesNotRescanRuntimeDataSummary|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testCompanionAppModelRetentionScheduleDoesNotKeepModelAlive|AetherLinkLocalizationTests/testRuntimeHistoryInspectorCopyLocalizesAcrossSupportedLanguages|AetherLinkRenderSmokeTests/testRuntimeHistoryInspectorRendersAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances'` passes 54 tests with no failures, including 49 SQLite tests. The model regression drains 105 sessions across two policy batches, the no-rescan regression fixes the summary-store list count after initialization, the lifetime regression releases the model while its periodic schedule exists, the in-process cross-instance race preserves a queued append, and the render regression covers all supported languages and appearances. No separate writer-process or active-child-cancellation timing test is claimed.
- Static evidence: all five `.strings` files pass `plutil -lint`; `git diff --check` passes for the bounded source/test/resource set. The default no-device gate explicitly includes bounded metadata SQL/targeted FTS, cross-instance append coordination, final-batch legacy compaction, app-model multi-batch/no-rescan/lifetime regressions, localization/render suites, and the `macOS runtime chat retention production ownership addendum`.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-macos-runtime-chat-retention-production-ownership-final-reviewed-20260714.log` has 9,185 lines and exits 0 with `No-device quality checks passed.`, 49 passing SQLite tests, the app-model no-summary-rescan regression, 761 encrypted relay frame bodies, the runtime chat retention production ownership addendum, four bounded Phase A controlled-spike approvals, and the socket gate closed.
- Review evidence: GPT-5.6 Sol identified retained legacy transcript content, once-per-lifetime/backlog truncation, SQLite all-event decoding/full-index rebuilds, concurrent append replacement loss, repeated full-file compaction, detached cancellation, main-actor full-history refresh, and evidence overclaim. The focused result validates the executed remediations within its stated scope. The implementation provides current-writer instance locking and a POSIX process lock, but only the instance race is executed here; a pre-protocol build must be stopped before migration, one legacy compaction remains proportional to the legacy file, and one synchronous SQLite batch finishes before cancellation is observed.
- Proof boundary: no-device macOS SwiftPM/storage/model/localization/render evidence only. It is not physical Android history UI, optical QR, live-provider chat, production relay/P2P, real-network behavior, libjuice source audit, C ABI compile, or production-network proof.
- Agent state: GPT-5.6 Sol only; GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used.

- Scope: no-device activation of approved runtime-shared semantic document ranking. Legacy lexical requests remain unchanged; live-provider quality, physical Android, citations, and client trusted-source review are outside this evidence.
- Router evidence: eight `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.testSemanticRetrieval*` tests cover explicit semantic wire shape and redaction, bounded snippets and deterministic ordinal ranks, strong-model cache reuse after SQLite reopen with a query-only second embedding, LM Studio atomic no-fingerprint inference, backend failure without lexical fallback, cancellation after document access, cancellation during final audit rollback, replacement/revocation during blocked inference, and zero-limit content-free audit. Existing lexical and serializer tests continue to prove omission compatibility.
- Approval/audit evidence: pre-inference SQLite revalidation records content-free `semantic_accessed` before candidate text reaches the backend; backend failure and cancellation observed before the final audit commit retain this access evidence without a `.queried` row. The final conditional transaction revalidates approval scope, full source revision, document/chunk identity, model input byte limit, and exact bounded-content fingerprint, removes stale candidates, and records `.queried` before response serialization. Query and body canaries are absent from both actions.
- Cache evidence: 19 `SQLiteRuntimeDocumentSemanticEmbeddingCacheTests` pass, including model input byte-limit separation beside strong model identity, approval visibility, deterministic document round-robin fairness, huge finite vector stability, reopen, invalidation, stale rollback, cancellation, contention, row/vector-byte ceilings, the 800-source approval ceiling, and fail-closed oversized legacy approval handling. Candidate discovery selects at most 200 usable documents, reads at most 800 chunk rows total without a windowed corpus sort, retains its progress-handler context until explicit unregister, and has direct regression coverage for cancellation from inside `sqlite3_step`. Query vectors have no storage API.
- Host management evidence: 12 `RuntimeDocumentSourceManagerTests` pass. `testHostManagementListsAndRevokesApprovedSourcesBeyondRemoteCatalogPage` creates 101 approvals, proves the host list exposes the source beyond the 100-row remote page, revokes it by current revision, and leaves the remote protocol catalog ceiling unchanged.
- Android evidence: `ProtocolCodecTest.retrievalQueryRequestSerializesEmbeddingModelHintAndRejectsBlankHint`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentSearchSendsSelectedEmbeddingModelHint`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentSemanticSearchRetriesLexicallyOnceAndIgnoresLateSemanticResponses`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentSearchIgnoresVeryLateErrorsAfterManyCompletedSearches`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentSemanticSearchDoesNotFallbackForNonCompatibilityErrors`, and the complete `runtimeDocument*` focused selection pass. They prove bounded opt-in, one exact older-runtime retry, terminal noncompatibility failures, and stale success/error isolation beyond the prior bounded retired-id window.
- Smoke and aggregate evidence: `xcrun swiftc -typecheck script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift` and `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --default-mock-routing-only` pass. The full smoke checks semantic cold fill, explicit redacted response, and query-only cache hit. `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-approved-semantic-document-retrieval-final-reviewed-20260712.log` records aggregate exit status 0, `No-device quality checks passed.`, 42 fresh relay connections, 716 encrypted frame bodies, and the semantic document retrieval addendum in the complete Android/Swift/protocol/docs/static-design queue.
- Independent review: GPT-5.6 Sol explorers defined the approval, batching, compatibility, and audit contract; Sol workers implemented focused Android and Swift tests. Review findings covering host management beyond the remote catalog page, bounded candidate SQL work, cancellation inside `sqlite3_step`, retained progress-handler context lifetime, and candidate-phase test precision have direct fixes and regressions. The final source re-review reports no remaining P0-P3 findings.

## 2026-07-11 Semantic Document Match-Kind And Revision-Keyed Cache Foundation

- Scope: no-device pre-activation contract and storage foundation. Production `retrieval.query` remains lexical and Android emits no document `embedding_model_id`; no semantic ranking or live provider call is claimed.
- Wire evidence: the shared schema keeps `match_kind` optional and closed to `lexical`/`semantic`, defaults omission to legacy lexical semantics, requires non-empty bounded terms for missing/lexical origin, and allows empty honest overlap only for explicit semantic origin. The schema checker accepts and rejects canonical samples for each branch.
- Android evidence: `ProtocolCodecTest.retrievalQueryMatchKindDefaultsLexicalAndControlsMatchedTermsBounds`, `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentSearchAcceptsSemanticMatchKindAndPreservesEmptyTerms`, and `ClientScreensNoDeviceComposeTest.settingsSemanticDocumentSearchUsesLocalizedMetadataAcrossSupportedLanguages` pass. The UI uses localized English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French semantic-origin metadata rather than placing a ranking kind inside the lexical terms field.
- Cache evidence: 12 `SQLiteRuntimeDocumentSemanticEmbeddingCacheTests` pass. They cover strong local-model fingerprint policy, approved-only bounded candidates, blank-row candidate fairness, reopen reuse, model separation, replacement/delete/filtered/delete-all transaction invalidation, concurrent cache/index writers, stale-revision rejection and full batch rollback, cancellation before commit, row and vector-byte eviction, invalid keys/vectors, and oversized read/write batch rejection.
- Storage boundary: the cache is a table in the document index database, not an owner-scoped sidecar. It stores candidate vectors only under document/chunk, approved revision, canonical model, strong model fingerprint, encoding version, and exact bounded-content fingerprint. Replacement and deletion purge rows before document/chunk removal in the same SQLite transaction.
- Focused commands: `swift test --filter SQLiteRuntimeDocumentSemanticEmbeddingCacheTests`, the selected Swift router and Android protocol/ViewModel/Compose tests, `python3 script/check_protocol_schema.py`, `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, both production security-design validators, `swift build --target CompanionCore`, and `git diff --check` pass.
- Independent review: GPT-5.6 Sol found normal SQLite writer contention without a busy timeout, statement cleanup on eviction failure, blank-row candidate starvation, delayed cancellation, unbounded cache reads, missing bulk/rollback tests, unsolicited future match-kind compatibility risk, whitespace-only terms, semantic-origin text inside the lexical terms label, and missing Swift wire regressions. Busy waiting, scoped statement cleanup, usable-candidate limits, per-iteration cancellation, symmetric 64-row limits, expanded transaction/concurrency tests, explicit serializer opt-in, nonblank terms, separate five-language metadata, and lexical/semantic wire regressions address them. Both final re-reviews report no remaining P0-P3.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-semantic-document-cache-foundation-final-reviewed-20260711.log` records exit status 0, `No-device quality checks passed.`, the Android retrieval match-kind and runtime document semantic-cache foundation addenda, 42 fresh relay connections, 710 encrypted frame bodies, and the complete Android/Swift/protocol/docs/static-design queue.

## 2026-07-11 macOS Host Document Source Review And Audit Export

- Scope: no-device macOS host UI and Core management path for explicit `runtime_shared` document source approval. This does not activate semantic retrieval, embeddings, citations, or a client approval namespace.
- Manager evidence: 11 `RuntimeDocumentSourceManagerTests` prove prepare does not approve, wrong or reused tokens fail, reviews expire and cancel, a newer review invalidates the prior in-memory candidate, replacement leaves the old approved revision readable until confirmation, atomic SQLite revision predicates prevent stale replacement and removal, a post-validation symlink swap fails at `O_NOFOLLOW`, random source ids remain stable across replacement, and export is exactly capped at the newest 1,000 events in newest-first order.
- Same-store evidence: `testCompanionAppModelPublishesOnlyReviewedSourcesFromInjectedRuntimeStore` proves the model keeps a candidate unpublished before approval, then publishes it through the same injected SQLite store used for governed remote reads, and removes it from both UI state and approved catalog.
- Snapshot/privacy evidence: import uses security-scoped coordinated access, rejects directories and symlinks, copies at most 32 MiB to a mode-`0600` app-private temporary snapshot, extracts there, and removes the snapshot. Errors are typed and path-free. The audit export regression asserts document text, source path, query, and snippet markers are absent.
- UI evidence: all 111 `AetherLinkLocalizationTests` pass, including typed management-error localization, distinct indexed/approved actions, filename-bound removal labels, and labeled review expiry. `testPrimaryCompanionSurfacesRenderAtMinimumDetailSizeAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances` covers the empty inspector, while `testPopulatedDocumentSourceInspectorAndReviewRenderAcrossLanguagesAndAppearances` covers long filenames, source and audit rows, replacement review, and accessibility text size across English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French in system, light, and dark appearances.
- Independent review: GPT-5.6 Sol found P1 non-atomic replace/remove revision checks, P2 pathname reopen after symlink validation, P3 inconsistent unavailable-governance detail, and UI P2/P3 action, localization, accessibility, expiry, and render gaps. Atomic SQLite predicates, `O_NOFOLLOW` plus `fstat`, generic route failures, localized target-aware UI, and expanded regressions address them. Final Core and UI re-reviews reported no remaining P0-P3 findings.
- Retention/export boundary: in-memory and SQLite audit storage retain the newest 100,000 events and trim only oldest overflow during insertion. The inspector previews 50 events and exports the latest 1,000 as sorted JSON without paths, bookmarks, bodies, queries, snippets, or pending candidate content.
- Aggregate evidence: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-host-document-review-final-reviewed-20260711.log` records exit status 0, `No-device quality checks passed.`, the host document review and audit export addendum, 42 fresh relay connections, 712 encrypted frame bodies, and the complete Android/Swift/protocol/docs/static-design queue.

## 2026-07-11 Runtime-Shared Document Source Governance Foundation

- Scope: no-device backend governance prerequisite for future semantic document retrieval. No semantic document embedding or ranking path is active.

The combined in-memory/SQLite/governance selection passes 73 tests.
- Router and concurrency evidence: `testApprovedRuntimeSharedDocumentsAuditTrustedReadersAndRevokeAcrossDevices` proves two authenticated devices can read the same approved shared source, catalog/query/anchor actions carry authenticated actor ids, query/body canaries are absent from audit, and revoke blocks retrieval plus stale anchors. `testDocumentReadsFailClosedWhenSourceAuditWriteFails` proves catalog, query, and anchor responses become `document_index_unavailable` when audit persistence fails. `testSQLiteApprovedQueryAndAuditLinearizeBeforeConcurrentRevocation` blocks at the read/audit boundary and proves revoke cannot complete until the approved query audit commits, after which later retrieval is empty. `testAcceptedZeroResultApprovedQueriesStillWriteAudit` proves punctuation-only and zero-limit successful queries still append a zero-result audit in both stores.
- Storage boundary: approvals are separate from legacy document rows and use a strong revision over metadata and complete normalized chunk content. SQLite approval/index/reindex/revoke/delete changes are transactional; audit is retained without query text, snippet, or body columns.
- Product boundary at this checkpoint: RuntimeDevServer was the only non-test ingestion caller. Newer sections above add production host approval, bounded audit export UI, the optional match-kind contract, and a bounded internal vector cache foundation. Semantic ranking, citation, and trusted-source review remain absent.

## 2026-07-11 Runtime Embedding-Backed Chat Semantic Search

- Scope: no-device validation of the first real runtime-host semantic ranking path for prior chat sessions using the selected local embedding model.
- Provider evidence: focused Ollama and LM Studio tests validate batch request shapes, Ollama no-truncation policy, LM Studio index-order restoration, wrong counts, duplicate/missing/out-of-range indexes, empty vectors, finite values, and dimension consistency.
- Aggregate evidence: focused tests prove provider-qualified installed local embedding models route to their provider model id, chat models are rejected, and embedding inference does not enter chat generation cancellation bookkeeping.
- Router/ranking evidence: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testChatSessionsListEmbeddingModelHintStaysSearchOnly` now proves the hint invokes `embed`, remains absent from the response, cannot override the session chat model, and preserves the existing search metadata shape. Separate tests prove provider failures do not silently fall back to lexical search and oversized/excessive-term queries fail before store search or embedding dispatch. `RuntimeSemanticChatSessionSearchTests` covers cosine ordering, malformed vector rejection, UTF-8 document bounds, and inline attachment-byte exclusion.
- Resource/privacy boundary: semantic inputs stay within the authenticated owner scope and are limited to 200 sessions, 100 messages per session, a model-context-derived candidate ceiling capped at 8,192 UTF-8 bytes with a conservative 1,024-byte fallback, 256 query characters, and 16 distinct terms. SQLite/JSONL derive all owner-scoped session/message sources from one event snapshot, newest messages receive the byte budget first, one connection can hold one search, global concurrency is capped at four, and connection close cancels the tracked request. Runtime-only context, compaction metadata, and inline attachment bytes are not embedded or returned.
- Development seam: `DevMockBackend` advertises `ollama:nomic-embed-text` and produces deterministic local mock embeddings. A dedicated default single-provider aggregate smoke and the authenticated relay aggregate smoke both execute actual semantic dispatch while checking provider-qualified ids, model-id/vector response redaction, chat-model isolation, and invalid model rejection.
- Focused result: the provider/aggregate/router/helper selection passes 54 tests, the post-integration semantic/resource selection passes 19 tests, the review-fix selection passes 18 tests, and all 277 `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests` pass. Coverage includes SQLite single-snapshot owner isolation, two authenticated devices producing disjoint embedding inputs, connection serialization/cancellation, a noncooperative successful embedding response after cancellation, active-chat close ordering, embedding residency transition, cloud embedding rejection, newest-message priority, model-aware byte limits, and default aggregate mock routing.
- Independent review: GPT-5.6 Sol completed three review passes. Findings covering event-log rereads, request concurrency, residency, provider context limits, truncation order, mock routing/model kind, semantic-slot lifetime, alias matching, and post-embedding cancellation were fixed with regressions; the final pass reported no remaining P1 issue and its two P2 gaps were closed by cancellation rechecks plus the dedicated default mock routing smoke.
- Final aggregate result: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" bash script/check_no_device_quality.sh` exited 0. `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-embedding-chat-semantic-final-reviewed-20260711.log` records `No-device quality checks passed.`, the runtime embedding-backed chat semantic search addendum, the default aggregate mock routing smoke, authenticated relay semantic dispatch, 42 fresh relay connections, 704 encrypted frame bodies, and the complete Android/Swift/protocol/docs/static-design queue after all review fixes.

## 2026-07-11 Budget-Safe Adaptive Chat Context Compaction

- Scope: no-device verification of model-window-aware runtime `chat.send` compaction, hard input-budget enforcement, backend-only provenance/summary role separation, request-bound source fingerprints, append-only effective terminal accounting, pre-backend rejection, compatibility fallback, and Android localized error presentation.
- Planner evidence: `RuntimeChatContextCompactionPlannerTests` covers exact unchanged requests, reserve and input-budget calculation, adaptive retention below the former 12-turn floor, multilingual/emoji accounting, deterministic estimation, runtime-system preservation, oversized newest-user rejection, contiguous oldest-prefix source pointers, and prompt-injection text remaining in an untrusted assistant message rather than generated system context.
- Router/store evidence: focused `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests` cover model-window-dependent compaction, byte- and decoded-image-aware `conservative_utf8_bytes_vision_framing_v2`, request-bound `adaptive_backend_only_summary_v3` accounting/source pointers, before/after budget bounds, visible-history separation, no backend call when mandatory input cannot fit, nonretryable `chat_context_window_exceeded`, and the legacy 24,000-character heuristic when model metadata omits `context_window_tokens`. Event-store validation requires complete internally consistent adaptive accounting plus one request-matching contiguous-prefix pointer. V3 additionally recomputes its canonical SHA-256 fingerprint and byte count from the stored compacted prefix; metadata contains no summary, source excerpt, or prompt-text field.
- Effective-accounting evidence: generated-summary and deterministic-fallback router regressions compare terminal `estimated_input_tokens_after` with a fresh estimate of the exact captured primary request. Prepass cancellation records `primary_dispatched=false` with no method or estimate. SQLite and JSONL roundtrips preserve valid resolution but keep method names out of FTS; malformed placement, method, budget, estimate, and undispatched shapes fail closed. The request event remains explicit planning provenance rather than being rewritten.
- Generated-summary evidence: focused router regressions require a bounded prepass on the same selected runtime-host model, removal of backend and inline reasoning, deterministic fallback for oversized output, atomic connection-owned cancellation of primary and derived `:compaction-summary` generations, persisted cancellation intent across the backend-generation handoff, lock-free external primary stream construction with registration-in-progress cancellation intent, and one active reservation namespace for both ids. `AggregatingLlmBackendResidencyTests.testCancelBeforeAsyncRouteResolutionPreventsProviderChatDispatch` proves the aggregate task is cancellable before provider selection and no provider chat dispatch follows; `testRejectedDuplicateGenerationCannotRemoveOriginalReservation` proves rejected duplicates cannot clean up or replace the original reservation. Additional regressions prove router collision rejection without context replacement and absence of generated summary text from SQLite chat history and session search. Planner tests keep generated output in the untrusted assistant role and reject blank or non-fitting replacements.
- Durable cache evidence: `SQLiteRuntimeChatCompactionSummaryCacheTests` covers a dedicated exact-source fingerprint domain, normalized owner/full-key isolation, owner-only reopen, transaction rollback when commit intent changes, corrupt-row misses, bounded deterministic eviction, invalid-input rejection, and scoped deletion. Router regressions prove a successful generated summary is reused after constructing a new cache/router instance without a second prepass, primary failure does not commit and therefore retries the prepass, and chat-session delete purges the derived owner/session rows. The resolved key uses the actual provider model id returned by model resolution, not the client alias. Generated summary text remains outside runtime chat events, `chat.messages.list`, and FTS; the sidecar is a derived runtime cache only.
- Android evidence: strict protocol decoding accepts `chat_context_window_exceeded`; the UI maps it to dedicated English, Korean, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and French resources. No-device Compose coverage verifies the localized banner and accessibility summary remain bounded at large font scale without displaying raw runtime detail.
- Storage boundary: client-visible request messages remain the stored transcript authority. Fixed runtime provenance, the untrusted backend-only historical summary, and their text do not enter `chat.messages.list` or session search indexes; only structural source pointers and accounting may accompany the stored request event.
- Compatibility boundary: missing context-window metadata keeps the existing 24,000-character and 12-recent-turn heuristic. This fallback and `conservative_utf8_bytes_vision_framing_v2` are conservative runtime estimates, not evidence of provider tokenizer parity.
- Independent review: GPT-5.6 Sol identified the unsafe three-byte text estimate, missing decoded-image cost, and insufficient adaptive source-pointer/event binding. The reviewed implementation now applies byte-level and decoded-pixel upper bounds, validates exactly one request-matching contiguous-prefix pointer for adaptive v2 while retaining legacy v1 compatibility, and has focused regressions for both findings.
- Terminal-accounting review: GPT-5.6 Sol identified that standalone terminal resolution shape validation did not durably bind the record to its source request. SQLite and JSONL now require a preceding normalized owner/session/request-scoped adaptive v3 request and matching estimator/input budget on append and reopen. Focused unbound, non-v3, mismatched, and raw-tamper regressions pass; the final re-review reported no P0-P3 findings.
- Aggregate result: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-chat-compaction-effective-resolution-final-reviewed-20260712.log` records exit status 0, `No-device quality checks passed.`, the adaptive budget, backend-only LLM summary, adaptive v3 source-fingerprint, and effective terminal accounting addenda, all 41 SQLite/JSONL chat-store tests, aggregate cancellation/reservation regressions, request-binding append/reopen validation, 48 fresh relay connections, 761 encrypted frame bodies, Android protocol/UI coverage, and static protocol/docs/security-design validation.
- Proof boundary: the phone is disconnected. This is no-device Swift/Kotlin/JVM/Compose and local-store evidence; it does not prove physical Android, live-provider tokenizer parity, production relay/P2P, optical QR, or real network connectivity.
- Post-review result: 12 local cache/router tests and 14 GPT-5.6 Sol focused tests pass after fixing both P2 findings for external backend invocation under `chatStorageLock` and client-alias cache keys. ## 2026-07-11 Review-Required Long-Inactivity Memory Summary Generation

## 2026-07-11 Relay Bounded Waiting And Authenticated Identity Fairness

- Scope: process-local unmatched room duration and post-proof identity-level waiting fairness for the development relay, layered after global/source accepted-socket protection.
- Defaults/configuration: one monotonic `60`-second room deadline capped by allocation lease expiry and `4` unmatched waits per role-separated authenticated identity across sources. Duration accepts `1...3600`; identity quota accepts `1...65536`; neither has a disable value.
- Identity boundary: runtime identity comes only from a revalidated runtime-key binding after proof, paired-client identity only from a pinned client binding after proof. Failed proofs are uncounted. Bootstrap clients without paired-client proof and legacy peers remain identity-untracked, source/global limited, and duration limited.
- Unit result: `swift test --filter RelayWaitingPeerPolicyTests` passes four tests for defaults/bounds, unauthenticated exclusion, role separation, cross-source atomic quota enforcement, exact release, source-free reasons/metrics, and saturation.
- Matcher result: five focused tests prove same-role replacement retains a live first deadline, allocation lease caps it, late counterpart matching and same-role replacement cannot use an expired room, readiness probes cannot report an expired runtime when timer delivery is delayed, a waiting attempt retains its atomic deadline after a counterpart moves the room active, identity quota is cross-source, different identities remain admissible, source rollback occurs on identity rejection, and every expiration/match/replacement/unregister/invalidation path releases exact counts.
- Socket result: five focused tests prove timeout EOF plus source/identity release and retry, active bridge timer cancellation with continued bidirectional forwarding, same-runtime identity rejection with another runtime identity admitted, paired-client proof identity fairness, and wrong/replayed runtime proof isolation. The exact focused slice passes 14 tests.
- Suite result: all 168 `RelayServerCoreTests` pass and `swift build --product AetherLinkRelay` passes.
- Operator result: wrapper/default/custom dry-run JSON and direct binary checks cover `--waiting-timeout-seconds`, `--max-waiting-peers-per-authenticated-identity`, matching environment variables, canonical decimal input, bounds, no-disable behavior, post-auth-only scope, and unauthenticated bootstrap source-only scope.
- Durable gate: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` runs the exact 14-test slice and operator guards; `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins matcher ordering, proof-before-identity construction, immutable deadline/lease cap, matcher-atomic expiration before registration/probe decisions, atomic deadline return without a post-publication lookup, timeout cancellation/release, tests, operator surfaces, source-free observability, and proof boundaries.
- Aggregate result: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" bash script/check_no_device_quality.sh` exited `0` and recorded `No-device quality checks passed.` in `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-relay-waiting-identity-final-20260711.log`. The artifact records the exact 14-test waiting/identity slice, all 168 `RelayServerCoreTests`, 110 localization tests under the stored non-English user preference, the 17-artifact production-relay validator, copy/docs hygiene, and the durable bounded-waiting summary.
- Proof boundary: the phone is disconnected. This is no-device unit/matcher/loopback evidence, not Sybil resistance, per-user isolation, carrier-NAT/VPN fairness measurement, production identity service, production capacity/load/latency, public-relay/live-network behavior, production TLS/KEX/pair-epoch implementation, optical QR, or physical Android proof.
- Agent state: GPT-5.6 Sol only; GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. Final Sol review found no remaining actionable finding; residual risk is limited to the absence of long-running socket stress/model checking and public-network/physical-device evidence.

## 2026-07-10 Relay Source Peer Quotas No-Device Gate

- Scope: canonical accepted-source concurrent connection admission and matcher-atomic unmatched waiting-peer admission for the development relay.
- Defaults/configuration: `64` connections and `32` waiting peers per source, canonical positive values in `1...65536`, no disable value, and mandatory `2 * waiting <= connections` counterpart headroom. Shared NAT/VPN users share quotas.
- Unit result: `swift test --filter RelaySourceQuotaLimiterTests` passes seven tests covering defaults/bounds, global and source precedence, exact release, waiting replacement ownership, concurrency overshoot prevention, pre-waiter global/source headroom, atomic waiting-insertion reservation validation, counterpart reserve admission/confirmation/nonmatch rejection, the global-one edge, source-free metrics, and saturation.

The exact focused slice passes 16 tests.
- Suite result: all 155 `RelayServerCoreTests` pass and `swift build --product AetherLinkRelay` passes.
- Aggregate result: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-relay-source-peer-quotas-20260710.log` records `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` completing with exit `0`, including the exact 16-test quota slice, all 155 relay-core tests, Android unit/build coverage, authenticated relay smoke, copy/docs hygiene, and the 17-artifact production-relay design validator.

## Current Rule

- Source code, tests, smoke commands, and explicitly referenced current artifacts are authoritative.
- Files under `artifacts/` are generated QA captures. Treat them as historical unless the latest relevant `docs/progress.md` entry names the file and explains the device/runtime state that produced it.
- Do not use an old screenshot or XML dump as proof that the current UI copy, route behavior, QR pairing, or localization is still correct.
- No-device evidence does not prove physical Android rendering, TalkBack/VoiceOver traversal, optical/camera QR scan reliability, live provider-backed chat/cancel or chat-complete, or real different-network runtime connectivity.
- New `artifacts/*.png` and `artifacts/*.xml` files are ignored by default so stale generated captures are not accidentally committed.

## 2026-07-10 Current Relay Allocation Cross-Process Ownership Evidence

### Relay Allocation Cross-Process Ownership No-Device Gate

- Transaction evidence: one stable mode-`0600` marker carries fixed-format `U`/`A`/`E` state and a 64-character lowercase hexadecimal coordination token bound into schema v4. POSIX `F_SETLK` byte range 0 serializes reload/compare-and-swap/persist, and byte range 1 is `RelayServer` lifetime ownership.
- Descriptor evidence: the process pool keys marker descriptors by inode, reuses its pooled descriptor, and retains only duplicates that cannot safely be closed because closing any descriptor for an `F_SETLK` file can release that process's locks. Blocking acquisition has a five-second monotonic deadline.
- Persistence evidence: descriptor-relative `openat`, `fstatat`, `renameat`, and `unlinkat` use `O_NOFOLLOW`; targets must be current-user-owned regular files with `nlink == 1` under a parent that is not group- or world-writable. Reconciliation uses an owner-only temporary file, file `fsync`, atomic rename, and directory `fsync`.
- Fail-closed evidence: a missing established store, dangling symlink, hard link, case/path alias, marker replacement, and marker/store token mismatch are rejected. A valid unversioned `rt1` store is recognized, then all leases are revoked into an empty token-bound schema-v4 store because identity cannot be migrated.
- Stale-process evidence: disjoint commits from stale registry instances merge. Stale same-ID create and stale paired-claim races each return one success and one `allocationConflict`; a stale create cannot restore a consumed bootstrap ID, and the winning pair binding and consumed-bootstrap tombstone persist atomically in one schema-v4 envelope. A token-matched schema-v4 store beside a `U` marker recovers after interrupted first initialization.
- Process-ownership evidence: a second relay process sharing the store fails before independent matcher or active-room state; same-instance concurrent `run()` calls do not release the live listener, and bind failure releases byte range 1. The actual-process simultaneous-first-start smoke converged to one owner, verified TCP acceptance, and started a successor after owner exit.
- Focused result: all 64 `RelayAllocationTests`, 21 relay socket tests, and 100 related relay tests passed with zero failures. Full `swift test` passed 797 tests.
- Aggregate result: the authenticated relay smoke passed with 41 connections and 688 encrypted frame bodies. `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-relay-cross-process-ownership-20260710.log` contains `No-device quality checks passed.` and the relay cross-process ownership coverage summary. Diagnostic wrapper checks use per-run stores and do not depend on the user's default allocation store.

## 2026-07-10 Pair-Scoped Relay Room Evidence (Previous Milestone)

### Pair-Scoped Relay Room Isolation No-Device Gate

- Contract evidence: Swift and Android use `runtime-client-p256-v2`, protocol version 2, and fixed role-separated digests `445ee1adc3d521b2ba9d09e39d1ab23e913a262e5a5619f813ab162abc6ec37a` and `fa37320c45fef6dfdea036fd0315262d9f067f51b0ce335f7a31890419d822fa`. The signed transcript includes `current_relay_id` and `next_relay_id`; claim rotates to the deterministic pair ID and renewal stays on it.
- Allocation evidence: schema v4 replaces the consumed bootstrap allocation with the pair record, persists a closed `consumed_bootstrap_allocations` tombstone, migrates schema-v3 paired records on renewal, and rejects bootstrap reuse, stale generation, wrong key, replay, and corrupt/unknown persistence.
- Admission evidence: `paired-client-p256-v1` challenges the persistent Android key before matcher insertion. Focused wire, Android transport, and relay socket tests cover valid proof, missing challenge downgrade, wrong/mutated/replayed/expired proof, and waiting-runtime preservation.
- Room evidence: active rooms reject duplicate pairs until bridge close, renewal closes stale waiting generations, and `testTwoPairScopedRoomsBridgeConcurrentlyWithoutCrossTalk` proves two pair IDs bridge simultaneously without cross-room frame delivery.
- macOS evidence: `PairScopedRelayRouteStoreTests` proves strict two-client persistence, secret-store separation, update/removal isolation, and corrupt-input fail-close. `PairedRuntimeRouteRefreshTests` proves per-client pair-route persistence and successful response completion before pair transport activation and consumed-bootstrap rotation.
- Android evidence: paired ViewModel tests prove deterministic next-ID validation before signing, client co-signature, final pair-ID/generation persistence, and renewal from the saved route. Real relay TCP integration performs `paired-client-p256-v1` admission before strict crypto confirmation during reconnect.
- Proof boundary: no-device unit, persistence, loopback socket, and mock integration evidence only. It is not a production deployment claim.
- Known limits: this evidence does not establish cross-process compare-and-swap/allocation-store ownership, allocation TLS/server authentication, service-signed final responses, server-side immediate active-session revocation, production P2P/NAT traversal, abuse controls, or full roadmap completion.
- Caveat: the physical Android phone is disconnected. No live-device reconnect, optical QR, live-provider, or real different-network evidence was produced.
- Focused results: the targeted Android paired-allocation/admission suite passed 8 tests, and full `swift test` passed 774 tests with zero failures.
- Relay smoke result: `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh` passed bootstrap operation checks, two trusted-device owner isolation, pair claim/renew, and pair-route client admission/reconnect. It observed 41 fresh session nonce/ephemeral-key/binding sets and 688 encrypted relay frame bodies.
- Full gate result: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-pair-scoped-relay-room-20260710.log` contains `No-device quality checks passed.` and `Covered pair-scoped relay room authorization addendum` after Android, Swift, protocol, schema, copy, docs, render, and shell guard coverage completed.
- Verification commands: targeted Gradle paired-allocation/admission tests, full `swift test`, `xcrun swiftc -typecheck script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift`, authenticated relay smoke, `python3 script/check_protocol_schema.py`, `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`, `python3 -m py_compile`, `bash -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh` (syntax only), `git diff --check`, and the full no-device quality gate passed.

## 2026-07-10 Paired-Device Relay Allocation Authorization Historical Gate

The earlier `runtime-client-p256-v1`/schema-v3 paired claim and renewal gate remains historical evidence. Its dated logs and test counts do not prove the current pair-room additions; the v2/schema-v4 gate above supersedes its global-room and unauthenticated-client-admission limitations.

## 2026-07-10 Initial Pairing Mutual P-256 Proof No-Device Gate

- Contract assertions: Kotlin and Swift share `p256-sha256-der-v1`, exact length-framed transcripts, canonical P-256 DER/base64/signature validation, QR-key fingerprints, and fixed client/result digest vectors.
- Trust-order assertions: the runtime verifies client key possession before reservation, signs before trusted-device persistence, rechecks the transport binding, and releases failed reservations. Android verifies request correlation, QR-pinned runtime identity, runtime signature, and live transport binding before trusted-runtime persistence.
- Fail-closed assertions: wrong keys, field mutation, request-id replay, downgrade, noncanonical DER/base64, missing QR runtime keys, signer failure, unsigned accepted results, and transport rebinding do not create trust. Unsigned rejections keep pending recovery material.
- Integration assertions: the Android real relay TCP fixture verifies the signed client request and returns a transport-bound signed result for both USB-reverse and private-overlay routes. RuntimeDevServer smoke verifies accepted result proof and a mutated-result rejection in direct and relay modes.
- Focused results: Android core pairing 108 tests, Android ViewModel 387 tests, Android relay integration 5 tests, Swift initial pairing 7 tests, and focused macOS pairing router 10 tests passed.
- Relay smoke result: direct and relay smoke passed; relay mode observed 40 fresh session nonce/ephemeral-key/binding sets and 670 encrypted frame bodies.
- Full gate result: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-initial-pairing-mutual-proof-20260710.log` contains `No-device quality checks passed.` and `Covered initial pairing mutual P-256 proof addendum`; relay smoke in the same run retained 40 fresh session nonce/key/binding sets and 670 encrypted frame bodies.
- Historical proof boundary for this earlier milestone: this no-device source/unit/loopback integration evidence did not prove optical QR, physical Android, live-provider, public different-network, paired-device allocation authorization, post-compromise recovery, or production deployment. The current paired-allocation section above supersedes only the no-device allocation-authorization gap.
- Caveat: the phone is disconnected.
- Verification commands: `./gradlew :core:pairing:testDebugUnitTest :core:protocol:testDebugUnitTest :app:testDebugUnitTest --no-daemon`, `swift test --filter 'InitialPairingProofTests|RuntimeIdentityKeyStoreTests|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests'`, `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh`, `python3 script/check_protocol_schema.py`, `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`, `bash -n script/check_no_device_quality.sh` (syntax only), `git diff --check`, and the full no-device quality gate passed.

## 2026-07-10 Runtime-Key-Bound Relay Allocation And Runtime-Role Admission Foundation (Previous Milestone)

- No-device assertions: the persistent runtime P-256 identity signs a same-socket allocation challenge; a successful `rt2` allocation ID is bound to the verified runtime-key fingerprint; and allocation ticket schema v2 persists that fingerprint plus a generation while required persistence failures fail closed.
- Admission assertions: runtime registration is admitted to matching only after verification of a signed transcript bound to the allocated lease, relay nonce, runtime session nonce, and runtime ephemeral P-256 key. Allocation preflight returns exactly `preflight`, `crypto_version`, and `allocation_auth`, with no route material.
- Runtime assertion: `LocalRuntimeMessageRouter` rejects an unstable or missing transport binding before pairing trust is persisted.
- Historical boundary at this milestone: the allocation bearer token was only a service gate and plaintext without outer TLS. Android did not yet co-sign allocation, so runtime-key allocation alone was not paired-device authorization; the guard-pinned conclusion was `not a full mutual identity-first key exchange`. The current gate above supersedes the co-signing gap, not the missing TLS/server-authentication or production-isolation gaps.
- Proof boundary: no-device source, unit, socket, persistence, preflight, matcher-admission, runtime-router, and authenticated relay-smoke evidence only.
- Caveat: the phone is disconnected, so physical-device, optical QR, live-provider, and real different-network proof are unavailable.
- Focused results: `RelayServerCoreTests` passed 68 tests, `LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests` passed 250 tests, `RelayPeerClientTests` passed 17 tests, `RuntimeIdentityKeyStoreTests` passed 13 tests, and `RelayIdentityAuthorizationTests` passed 4 tests.
- Relay smoke result: `build/qa/runtime-authenticated-mock-relay-runtime-key-admission-20260710.log` records successful signed allocation/admission, 40 fresh session nonce/ephemeral-key/transport-binding sets, and 670 encrypted frame bodies.
- Harness isolation: the loopback authenticated smoke uses an explicit ephemeral allocation registry, while no-ADB local-relay smoke uses a work-directory schema-v2 store. Neither silently falls back to a legacy or corrupt user-home registry.

## 2026-07-10 Endpoint-Owned Relay Secret Allocation V2 No-Device Gate

- Allocation wire evidence: strict parser and socket tests accept only canonical `crypto=2` allocation requests and exact secret-free responses containing `relay_id`, `relay_expires_at`, `relay_nonce`, and integer `crypto_version=2`.
- Fail-closed evidence: missing/extra response fields, service-returned `relay_secret`, wrong crypto versions, versionless requests, requested-secret requests, auth aliases, duplicate/reordered options, and malformed preflight options are rejected. A rejected legacy request leaves no derived relay ID usable for strict crypto-v2 registration.
- Endpoint evidence: macOS allocation clients return service metadata without any secret field, generate a cryptographically random 32-byte base64 secret when needed, reuse one preferred secret across endpoint fallback and lease renewal, and attach it locally before QR/route-refresh storage. RuntimeDevServer follows the same metadata-then-local-attach path.
- Relay-state evidence: crypto-v2 allocation tickets persist only opaque relay ID, expiry, and nonce. Preflight does not persist a ticket. Allocation-required server logs report only redacted/short identifiers and never receive endpoint traffic secrets.
- Focused results: `swift test --filter RelayServerCoreTests` passed 71 tests; the macOS allocation/app focused set passed 63 tests; the explicit five endpoint-allocation tests passed; `swift build --target RuntimeDevServer` passed; and `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py` passed after the endpoint-owned invariant was added.
- Relay smoke result: `build/qa/runtime-authenticated-mock-relay-endpoint-owned-allocation-v2-20260710.log` records success plus 40 fresh session nonce/ephemeral-key/binding sets and 670 encrypted frame bodies.
- Full gate result: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-endpoint-owned-relay-secret-v2-20260710-125039.log` contains `No-device quality checks passed.` and the endpoint-owned allocation v2 coverage summary.
- Verification commands: `swift test --filter RelayServerCoreTests`, the focused `swift test --filter 'LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/(testEnvironmentRemoteRelayRouteAllocatorReusesPreferredEndpointSecretWithoutPassingItToService|testEnvironmentRemoteRelayRouteAllocatorGeneratesThirtyTwoByteEndpointSecret|testTCPRelayServiceRouteAllocatorSendsExactV2RequestAndReturnsSecretFreeMetadata|testTCPRelayServiceRouteAllocatorRejectsSecretAndAnyExtraV2ResponseFields|testTCPRelayServiceRouteAllocatorRequiresClosedV2ResponseShape)'`, `swift build --target RuntimeDevServer`, `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh`, `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`, and `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./script/check_no_device_quality.sh` passed.
- Proof boundary: no-device source, unit, loopback socket, preflight, and authenticated RuntimeDevServer relay-smoke evidence only.
- Caveat: no Android device was attached. This evidence does not cover optical QR scanning, physical pairing/reconnect, a real public relay, real different-network reachability, production allocation identity/authentication, P2P traversal, or production end-to-end deployment.

## 2026-07-10 Strict Allocated Relay Crypto V2 No-Device Gate

- Cross-language contract: Android and Swift match fixed P-256 scalar-one/scalar-two public keys, the canonical crypto v2 transcript binding, HKDF-SHA256 confirmation/client/runtime secrets, both role proofs, directional frame-zero ciphertext, and the 65,536-frame epoch boundary.
- Handshake evidence: focused tests require exact `crypto=2` registration/registered/ready metadata, canonical fresh session nonces, canonical on-curve P-256 points at endpoints, mutual confirmation before ciphers activate, and no strict v1/plain downgrade. Relay socket tests prove the relay shape-validates metadata and forwards confirmation/frame bytes opaquely.
- Frame evidence: separate client/runtime traffic secrets derive AES-GCM keys in 65,536-frame epochs. Authentication failure and ordered replay do not advance receive state, wrong direction fails, and the maximum signed 64-bit frame index fails before cryptography rather than wrapping.
- Paired-identity evidence: the confirmed crypto v2 transcript binding remains in `hello`, `auth.challenge`, `auth.response`, and accepted authentication signatures. Missing/mismatched bindings, v1 signature downgrade, and old-binding replay remain rejected before command dispatch.
- Compatibility evidence: local direct behavior and legacy unallocated/plaintext relay registration, ready lines, framing, and v1 authentication bytes remain unchanged; strict and legacy relay peers reject cross-mode handshakes.
- Relay smoke: `build/qa/runtime-authenticated-mock-relay-crypto-v2-20260710.log` records exit 0, fresh session nonces/ephemeral keys/bindings across 40 connections, and 670 encrypted frame bodies without protected plaintext markers.
- Full gate: `build/qa/check-no-device-quality-relay-crypto-v2-20260710-121417.log` records `No-device quality checks passed.`, the 40-connection freshness marker, the 670 encrypted-frame marker, and the strict allocated relay crypto v2 gate summary.
- Verification commands: `JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :core:transport:testDebugUnitTest --tests com.localagentbridge.android.core.transport.RuntimeRelayTcpClientTest -Pkotlin.incremental=false --console=plain`, `swift test --filter 'ProtocolCodecTests|RelayPeerClientTests|RelayHandshakeTests|RelayMatcherTests|RelayServerSocketTests'`, `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --relay --expect-p2p-route-refresh`, `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`, and `./script/check_no_device_quality.sh`.
- Proof boundary: no-device cross-language contract, endpoint/relay loopback transport, paired-identity binding, authenticated mock runtime, and durable gate evidence only.
- Caveat: PSK-mixed ephemeral P-256 ECDH provides forward secrecy against later compromise of only the QR relay secret if ephemeral private keys were not retained. Endpoint-owned allocation now keeps that secret out of the default allocation-required relay, but this is not protection against a leaked endpoint/QR secret, post-compromise security, a complete unordered replay window, production paired-device allocation authorization, production end-to-end deployment proof, optical QR proof, or physical Android/different-network proof.

Runtime and client signatures use binding-aware v2 domain separation; missing or mismatched bindings, v1 downgrade signatures, and replay from a previous binding are rejected before command dispatch.
- Compatibility evidence: unbound local direct and legacy plaintext paths preserve the exact v1 runtime challenge and client response byte strings.

## 2026-07-10 Android Mixed-Route Relay-Fallback Retry No-Device Gate

- No-device focused evidence: mixed complete P2P and relay route material retains P2P-first connection ordering while the route-refresh scheduler follows the nearest active and retryable leases independently of the active transport.
- Physical/live-provider separation: the Android phone is disconnected for this slice; no Android app was installed or driven on hardware, no optical QR scan was attempted, and no Ollama or LM Studio provider was invoked.
- Result: P2P connection fails and relay succeeds; the minimum-window P2P lease still triggers immediate `route.refresh` on the authenticated relay session. A retryable failure uses the 30-second relay lease, sends no request before `ROUTE_REFRESH_RETRY_DELAY_MS`, and sends a distinct second request at the boundary.

## 2026-07-09 Android Unsolicited Source-Anchor Resolver Future-Metadata Boundary No-Device Gate

- No-device focused evidence: Android still treats unsolicited `source_anchor.resolve` as non-consumed runtime traffic even when the payload carries future/private resolver metadata.
- Physical/live-provider separation: the Android phone is disconnected for this slice; no Android app was installed or driven on hardware, no optical QR scan was attempted, and no Ollama or LM Studio provider was invoked.
- Result: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeIgnoresUnsolicitedSourceAnchorResolveResultWithoutAdvertisingOrPersisting` injects a raw unsolicited resolver result with `chunk_text`, `snippet`, `source_path`, `retrieval_context`, `citations`, `trusted_source`, `approval_state`, and `backend_url` canaries. Android keeps `source_anchor.resolve` out of `client_capabilities`, sends no resolver requests, preserves existing transient `retrieval.query` rows, leaves local persistence unchanged, and keeps those canaries out of later `chat.send`.
- Guardrail: this keeps Android resolver support at DTO parity only. It does not add Android UI resolver consumption, local persistence, chat context injection, source approval, citations, trusted-source review, permission/audit semantics, semantic retrieval, or direct backend access.
- Proof boundary: this proves no-device Android JVM ViewModel behavior only.
- Caveat: this is not physical Android proof; physical Android rendering on hardware, optical QR scanning, live phone pairing, live-provider chat/cancel, production relay/session/encryption, direct Android backend access, semantic retrieval, source approval, citations, trusted-source review, permission/audit semantics, and real different-network connectivity remain out of scope.

- Result: `RuntimeClientViewModelTest.runtimeDocumentSearchClearsTransientResultsAndSourceAnchorsOnDisconnect` proves a `retrieval.query` response can populate `documentSearchQuery`, `documentSearchResults`, and a canonical `source_anchor_id` before disconnect, then explicit disconnect and receive failure clear `documentSearchQuery`, `documentSearchResults`, and `source_anchor_id` so stale source-anchor values do not remain in transient state.
- Guardrail: this keeps source-anchor handles connection-scoped and transient. It does not add resolver protocol exposure, source approval, citation, trusted-source review, permission, audit, local persistence, semantic retrieval, or chat context behavior.
- Proof boundary: this proves no-device Android ViewModel disconnect cleanup only.
- Caveat: this is not physical Android proof; physical Android rendering on hardware, optical QR scanning, live phone pairing, live-provider chat/cancel, production relay/session/encryption, direct Android backend access, semantic retrieval, source approval, citations, trusted-source review, permission/audit semantics, and real different-network connectivity remain out of scope.

- Physical/live-provider separation: the Android phone is disconnected for this slice; no Android app was installed or driven, no optical QR scan was attempted, and no Ollama or LM Studio provider was invoked.
- Result: `index.documents.list` is now an active read-only protocol message that returns runtime-owned document catalog fields (`id`, `display_name`, `mime_type`, `content_fingerprint`, `extracted_character_count`, `chunk_count`, `quality`) plus aggregate summary counts. The route is authenticated, uses the existing runtime router limit bounding, and reads through a narrow `RuntimeDocumentIndexCatalogReading` seam implemented by both in-memory and SQLite document-index stores.
- Guardrail: the focused router tests prove catalog responses omit chunk text, chunk IDs, source paths, workspace/project IDs, retrieval context, embeddings, citations, and trusted-source metadata, and that response-only catalog payloads or future source metadata are rejected before document-index store dispatch. Protocol-schema hygiene permits only this read-only `index.` catalog message while keeping unsupported `index.*`, `retrieval.*`, embeddings, research, citations, source-control, permissions, and audit semantics reserved.
- Proof boundary: this proves only authenticated runtime document-index catalog listing and schema parity. It is not document chunk retrieval, semantic retrieval, embedding generation, citation generation, trusted-source approval, project/workspace registration, runtime file access, Android UI proof, optical QR proof, production relay/session/encryption proof, direct Android backend access proof, live-provider behavior proof, or real different-network connectivity proof.
- Agent state: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. GPT-5.5 read-only explorer `Peirce the 2nd` reviewed the next-roadmap candidates and recommended the catalog read path before retrieval.
- Caveat: later work still needs retrieval policy, source approval, citations, Android UI integration, runtime permission/audit semantics, and production transport boundaries before Workspace/RAG can be treated as user-facing retrieval.

## 2026-07-08 Runtime Document Index Requested Document ID Control-Character Canonicality No-Device Gate

- No-device focused evidence: `RuntimeDocumentIndexStoreTests` and `SQLiteRuntimeDocumentIndexStoreTests` cover control-character requested document IDs for runtime document-index lookup, maintenance, and storage APIs in the SwiftPM `CompanionCore` target.
- Physical/live-provider separation: the Android phone is disconnected for this slice; no Android app was installed or driven, no optical QR scan was attempted, and no Ollama or LM Studio provider was invoked.
- Result: `RuntimeDocumentIndexStore` rejects requested document IDs containing control characters before lookup, chunk reads, chunk metadata summaries, deletion, or storage. The shared canonicalizer feeds the in-memory store and SQLite store, so forged control-character requested IDs fall back to deterministic stable document IDs and do not persist in document rows, chunk rows, reopened SQLite rows, or raw SQLite FTS `document_id` columns.

- No-device focused evidence: `RuntimeDocumentIndexStoreTests` and `SQLiteRuntimeDocumentIndexStoreTests` cover chunk-envelope canonicality for runtime document-index chunk storage, chunk reads, chunk summaries, stable chunk IDs, and reopened SQLite rows in the SwiftPM `CompanionCore` target.
- Physical/live-provider separation: the Android phone is disconnected for this slice; no Android app was installed or driven, no optical QR scan was attempted, and no Ollama or LM Studio provider was invoked.
- Result: `RuntimeDocumentIndexStore` and `SQLiteRuntimeDocumentIndexStore` derive stored chunk indexes and offsets through store-owned envelopes. The tests prove forged chunk index and offset values from direct-ingestion chunks do not persist as stored `chunk_index`, `start_character_offset`, or `end_character_offset` values. Valid offsets must match document text, malformed offsets are located from document text when possible, and unlocatable forged chunk text receives bounded fallback offsets.

- Physical/live-provider separation: no Android app was installed or driven, no optical QR scan was attempted, and no Ollama or LM Studio provider was invoked.
- Result: `DocumentChunker` maps `ExtractedDocument` to deterministic bounded chunks with document file name, MIME type, index, character offsets, and text. ## 2026-07-08 Runtime Compaction Metadata Validation No-Device Gate

- No-device focused evidence: `SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests.testSQLiteStoreRejectsInvalidRuntimeCompactionMetadata` exercises the SQLite runtime chat event-store validation path without Android, optical QR, or live providers.
- Physical/live-provider separation: no Android app was installed or driven, no camera QR scan was attempted, and no Ollama or LM Studio backend was invoked for this proof.
- Result: SQLite runtime chat storage now has regression coverage proving malformed runtime-only compaction metadata is rejected before storage when it appears on non-request events, has a blank strategy, omits source pointers, carries blank source kind, has inconsistent compacted turn ranges, overlaps retained and compacted ranges, or exceeds total visible turns.
- Guardrail: `script/check_no_device_quality.sh` reports `runtime compaction metadata validation addendum`; `script/check_copy_hygiene.py` pins the focused test and rejection reasons.
- Proof boundary: this proves the structural runtime event-store metadata guard only. It is not Android UI proof, optical QR proof, production relay/session/encryption proof, live-provider behavior proof, direct Android backend access proof, or real different-network connectivity proof.
- Agent state: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. A GPT-5.5 read-only explorer recommended a separate document chunker follow-up; this focused storage validation patch was implemented locally.
- Caveat: tokenizer-aware budgets, LLM-generated summaries, richer durable compacted-session summaries, and production route/session work remain separate roadmap items.

Verification commands:

- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests/testSQLiteStoreRejectsInvalidRuntimeCompactionMetadata`

- Result: `PairingQrScanResult.kt` now owns the scanner raw-value batch result helper. It ignores blank/null frame values, returns a valid AetherLink route QR as soon as one is present, returns invalid-pairing feedback for malformed or route-less AetherLink pair QR values when no valid QR exists, and returns unsupported feedback only for non-AetherLink QR values.
- Result: `MainActivity` now passes ML Kit `Barcode.rawValue` strings into the helper instead of keeping the frame-batch priority logic private inside the camera analyzer.
- Proof boundary: this proves deterministic decoded-result classification and parser-policy reuse only. It is not optical/camera QR scan proof, physical Android rendering proof, live phone pairing proof, production relay/session/encryption proof, direct Android backend access proof, or real different-network connectivity proof.
- Agent state: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. A GPT-5.5 read-only explorer recommended this scanner decoded-result seam; implementation and verification were done locally.
- Caveat: a real camera scan can still fail before this helper if the QR is not optically decoded, the camera permission is denied, focus/exposure is poor, or the generated QR does not contain complete remote-route material.

## 2026-07-08 RuntimeDevServer Ollama Provider Eval Matrix And Whitespace Delta Store Fix

- No-device focused evidence: focused Swift tests cover JSONL and SQLite chat-store whitespace-only streaming chunks without a connected Android app. This is separate from the default no-device gate.
- Live-provider evidence: `script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --real-ollama --real-ollama-eval-models "gemma4:e4b-mlx,gemma4:26b-mlx,qwen3.6:35b-mlx" --eval-summary-json build/qa/runtime-provider-eval-ollama-matrix-20260708-035945.json` passed through authenticated RuntimeDevServer against live Ollama.
- Device state: adb observed attached phone `R3CXC0M76VM` / `SM_S936N`, but this evidence did not automate the Android app and is not physical Android UI or optical QR proof.
- Result: the failed earlier run exposed a real stream-storage bug where whitespace-only `assistant_delta` or `reasoning_delta` chunks were rejected as empty, causing `chat_store_unavailable`.
- Result: runtime chat storage now accepts non-empty whitespace-only answer and reasoning deltas so streamed spaces and reasoning newlines are preserved, while empty string deltas still fail closed.
- Result: `build/qa/runtime-provider-eval-ollama-matrix-20260708-035945.log` and `build/qa/runtime-provider-eval-ollama-matrix-20260708-035945.json` record `success=true`, authenticated runtime mediation, and redacted proof-boundary fields.
- Matrix: `gemma4:e4b-mlx`, `gemma4:26b-mlx`, and `qwen3.6:35b-mlx` each passed `korean_local_runtime_summary`, `runtime_boundary_explanation`, and `structured_json_boundary`; all nine evals had expected terms observed, `finish_reason=stop`, answer deltas, and reasoning deltas.
- Proof boundary: this is RuntimeDevServer-mediated live Ollama evidence, not direct-provider-only evaluation, Android client proof, LM Studio proof, production relay proof, production session-key exchange proof, production transport encryption proof, or real different-network proof.
- Agent state: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark was not used. A GPT-5.5 explorer reviewed the failure path and proof boundary.
- Caveat: this does not prove physical Android pairing or rendering, optical/camera QR scanning, LM Studio-backed live chat/cancel, production relay behavior, production session-key exchange, production transport encryption, or real different-network runtime connectivity.

Verification commands:

- `swiftc -typecheck script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift`
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter 'SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests|LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests/testRuntimeChatStorePreservesWhitespaceOnlyStreamingDeltas'`
- `./script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift --real-ollama --real-ollama-eval-models "gemma4:e4b-mlx,gemma4:26b-mlx,qwen3.6:35b-mlx" --eval-summary-json build/qa/runtime-provider-eval-ollama-matrix-20260708-035945.json`
- `python3 script/check_docs_hygiene.py`
- `python3 script/check_copy_hygiene.py`
- `git diff --check -- docs/progress.md docs/qa-evidence.md docs/roadmap.md apps/macos/CompanionCore/Sources/RuntimeChatEventStore.swift apps/macos/CompanionCore/Tests/LocalRuntimeMessageRouterTests.swift apps/macos/CompanionCore/Tests/SQLiteRuntimeChatEventStoreTests.swift script/runtime_authenticated_mock_smoke.swift`

## 2026-07-08 Physical External Relay Android Pairing Summary Artifact No-Device Gate

- No-device focused evidence: `script/check_physical_external_relay_pairing.sh` now passes a derived `<summary>.android-pairing-summary.json` path to the child Android pairing smoke and records safe child-summary proof booleans in the wrapper summary.

- Result: wrapper summaries now expose `android_pairing_summary_json_present`, `android_pairing_summary_success`, `android_pairing_summary_external_relay_mode`, `android_pairing_summary_no_relay_adb_reverse`, and `android_pairing_summary_proof_boundary_preserved` separately from live Android probe and physical external-relay proof.
- Result: the safe child summary subset includes only success, event counts, path-presence booleans, and proof-boundary booleans, keeping route material, provider/backend URLs, allocation tokens, relay secrets, and pairing URI query fields out of durable evidence.

It does not prove physical external relay reachability, a phone on a different network, optical/camera QR scanning, LM Studio-backed live chat/cancel, production relay behavior, production session-key exchange, production transport encryption, or real different-network runtime connectivity.
